searchable.name: Apetamin Syrup

searchable.description:
Apetamin Syrup belongs to a group of medicines called antihistamines. It is used in the treatment of various allergic conditions. It relieves symptoms of inflammation as well as itching, swelling, and rashes. It is also used to treat loss of appetite (need for food). It is an effective hunger stimulant also.

Apetamin should be used with caution in Kidney or liver impairment.
searchable.how_to_use:
Take this medicine in the dose and duration as advised by your doctor.

Check the label for directions before use. Measure it with a measuring cup and take it by mouth.

Shake well before use. Apetamin Syrup may be taken with or without food, but it is better to take it at a fixed time.

I
searchable.famasi_advice:

searchable.name: Dynamogen solution
searchable.brief_info: Dynamogen Oral Solution is a powerful combination tonic and appetite stimulant, trusted for restoring energy, fighting fatigue, and improving nutritional intake in both children and adults.
searchable.description: Dynamogen Oral Solution is a dual-action oral tonic provided in convenient 10 mL drinkable ampoules. It is scientifically formulated to combat the symptoms of physical and mental exhaustion (asthenia and psychoasthenia) while strongly promoting appetite (orexigenic action). The solution combines an appetite-stimulating agent with a vital amino acid, targeting both the restoration of basic energizing elements and the suppression of appetite loss. Dynamogen Oral Solution is a recommended treatment for individuals suffering from malnutrition, slow growth, school fatigue, and during periods of convalescence, helping to restore vitality and promote healthy weight gain.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Dynamogen Oral Solution

Dynamogen Oral Solution is a medicinal product subject to medical prescription in many regions.

Take as directed by the pharmacist or physician.

Typical Dosage:

  • Children: 1 drinkable ampoule twice daily, preferably taken half an hour before main meals. For severe cases of anorexia or weight deficits, 3 drinkable ampoules per 24 hours may be recommended.
  • Adults: Up to three drinkable ampoules per day.

Proper Administration

  • Route: Oral liquid (drinkable ampoule).
  • How to Take: Separate the ampoule, tear the upper tab by rotating, introduce the ampoule upside down into a glass, and press out the content. Consume the entire liquid content.
  • Timing: Preferably taken half an hour before main meals to maximize appetite stimulation.

Missed Dose Guidance

  • If you miss a dose, take it as soon as you remember before the next meal.
  • Do not take a double dose to make up for a missed one. Continue with the next scheduled dose before the appropriate meal.
searchable.specification: The active ingredients are Glutodine (Cyproheptadine alpha-Ketoglutarate) 3 mg and Arginine Aspartate 1 g per 10 mL ampoule. It is supplied as an oral solution in 10 mL drinkable ampoules (typically in packs of 20). It is a medicinal product subject to medical prescription.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Dynamogen Oral Solution

Do NOT use Dynamogen Oral Solution if you:

  • Have a known allergy or hypersensitivity to Cyproheptadine, Arginine Aspartate, or any other component of the solution (including excipients like Saccharose or Sorbitol).
  • Are a nursing mother or treating premature or newly born children.
  • Are children below two (2) years of age.
  • Are suffering from severe liver or kidney conditions (use is not recommended, though not strictly contraindicated).

Pregnancy & Breastfeeding Warnings

  • Pregnancy: Consult your doctor. As a precaution, the use of Dynamogen Oral Solution is not recommended in pregnancy due to the Cyproheptadine content.
  • Breastfeeding (Nursing Mothers): This product must not be used by nursing mothers due to its Cyproheptadine alpha-ketoglutarate content.

Side Effects of Dynamogen Oral Solution

Side effects are primarily related to the antihistamine component (Glutodine).

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Slight somnolence (drowsiness), which usually subsides after continuous use for some days. Dryness of the mouth, dizziness, blurred vision, constipation, or diarrhoea (which may be linked to the Sorbitol content).
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (that may require medical attention):
    • CNS Effects: Massive ingestion, particularly in children, may cause depression or stimulation of the central nervous system, and even convulsions.
    • Allergic Reactions: May include allergic reactions (due to coloring agents like Amaranth).

What to do if side effects occur:

  • If slight somnolence occurs, do not drive or operate machinery. If it persists beyond a few days, consult your doctor.
  • If you or a child experiences signs of severe CNS effects (confusion, stimulation, convulsions) or a severe allergic reaction (difficulty breathing), seek urgent hospital assistance immediately.
  • In case of accidental massive ingestion, induce vomiting after drinking a glass of water and seek hospital assistance.

Precautions and Warnings of Dynamogen Oral Solution

  • Drowsiness/Alertness: Be aware of the potential for slight drowsiness. Administer cautiously if the patient is performing tasks requiring mental alertness.
  • Anticholinergic Effects: Administer cautiously in aged patients and those with symptomatic prostate hypertrophy or narrow-angle glaucoma, as the anticholinergic effects of Cyproheptadine may be problematic.
  • Excipient Warnings: The solution contains saccharose and sorbitol. Diabetic patients and patients with hereditary fructose intolerance or glucose/galactose malabsorption must consider this. Sorbitol may cause upset stomach and diarrhoea.
  • Drug Interactions: Do not use simultaneously with MAO inhibitors. Avoid co-administration with other anticholinergic drugs, neuroleptics, tricyclic antidepressants, hypnotics, or major tranquilizers, as this may lead to additive side effects (enhanced drowsiness or anticholinergic effects).
  • Alcohol: It may increase the effect of alcohol ingestion. Avoid consuming alcohol while taking this medication.

searchable.name: Astyfer Syrup
searchable.brief_info: Astyfer Liquid is an amino acid and iron formulation used for anemias and amino acid supplementation in pregnancy, illness, or as a nutritional supplement
searchable.description: Astyfer Syrup is a specialized, blood-building nutritional supplement designed to treat and prevent iron deficiency anemia. Formulated as a "gentle" iron preparation, Astyfer combines iron with essential amino acids and a comprehensive B-vitamin complex. Unlike traditional iron salts that can cause stomach irritation, Astyfer is designed for maximum absorption and high tolerability. It is a trusted choice for individuals requiring increased iron intake, including growing children, pregnant women, and those recovering from surgery or chronic illness.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Astyfer Syrup This is a nutritional supplement. Take as directed by the pharmacist or physician.

  • Adults: Usually 5ml to 10ml (1 to 2 teaspoons) twice daily.
  • Children: Dosage is typically 5ml once or twice daily depending on age.
  • Administration: Shake well before use. Use a proper measuring cup for accuracy.
  • Timing: For the absolute best absorption, take on an empty stomach. However, if stomach upset occurs, it may be taken with or after food.

How to Store and Dispose of Astyfer Syrup

  • Storage: Store in a cool, dry place (below 30°C) away from direct sunlight. Ensure the cap is tightly closed.
  • Disposal: Keep out of reach of children. Return unused syrup to a pharmacy to prevent accidental ingestion by minors.

Quick Tips for Astyfer Syrup

  • Vitamin C Boost: Take your dose with a glass of orange juice; Vitamin C significantly increases iron absorption.
  • Avoid Tea/Coffee: Do not consume tea, coffee, or milk within an hour of taking Astyfer, as they contain tannins and calcium that inhibit iron uptake.
  • Stain Prevention: To prevent teeth staining, you can dilute the syrup in water or juice and drink it through a straw.
  • Consistency: Take it at the same time every day to maintain steady blood-building activity.
searchable.specification:
• Pack Size: 200ml bottle.
  • Form: Liquid Syrup.
  • Flavor: Often available in a pleasant fruit or orange flavor.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Astyfer Syrup

  • Iron Overload: Do NOT use if you have conditions like hemochromatosis or hemosiderosis.
  • Allergies: Avoid if you have a known hypersensitivity to iron salts or any of the B-vitamins.
  • Non-Iron Anemias: Should not be used to treat hemolytic anemia unless an iron deficiency is also present.

Side Effects of Astyfer Syrup

  • Common: Darkened stools (a harmless result of unabsorbed iron), mild constipation, or nausea.
  • Rare: Abdominal cramps or diarrhea.
  • Action: If you experience severe stomach pain or signs of an allergic reaction (rash/swelling), discontinue use and consult your doctor.

Precautions and Warnings

  • Existing Conditions: Use with caution if you have a history of peptic ulcers or inflammatory bowel disease.
  • Liquid Caution: Iron syrups can occasionally cause temporary staining of the teeth.
  • Drug Interactions: Do not take within 2 hours of antacids or calcium supplements, as these can block iron absorption.

searchable.name: Berocca Effervescent Tablets Orange Flavour
searchable.brief_info: Berocca Effervescent Tablets deliver a high-potency blend of 12 essential vitamins and minerals, trusted to support sustained energy release, reduce tiredness and fatigue, and contribute to normal mental and immune performance.
searchable.description: Berocca Effervescent Tablets Orange Flavour is a premium multivitamin and mineral supplement designed for adults and adolescents (typically 12 years and older) who face demanding schedules and require daily energy and focus support. The tablets dissolve quickly in water, creating a refreshing, great-tasting, sparkling orange drink. The formula is unique because it is free from sugar, caffeine, and artificial stimulants, relying instead on a high dose of B-Complex Vitamins to unlock energy from food. By supporting energy metabolism and neurological function, Berocca helps enhance physical stamina, improve concentration, and reduce the symptoms of tiredness and fatigue throughout the day.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Berocca Effervescent Tablets Orange Flavour

Berocca Effervescent Tablets is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) dietary supplement.

Adults and adolescents (over 12 years): Take 1 tablet dissolved in water once daily.

Proper Administration

  • Route: Oral liquid (effervescent solution).
  • How to Take: Drop one tablet into a full glass of water (approx. 200 ml to 300 ml) and wait for it to fully dissolve and stop fizzing. Drink the entire liquid content immediately.
  • Timing: Best taken early in the morning (e.g., with or after breakfast) to maximize energy support throughout the day and avoid potential stimulation near bedtime.

Missed Dose Guidance

  • If you miss a dose, simply take the next tablet the following morning. Consistency is key for energy and immune support. Do not take a double dose.
searchable.specification: The active ingredients include 8 B-Vitamins, Vitamin C (500 mg), Magnesium (100 mg), Calcium (100 mg), and Zinc (10 mg). It is supplied as an orange-flavoured effervescent tablet in a tube (common sizes are 10, 15, 30, or 45 tablets). It is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) Multivitamin Supplement.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Berocca Effervescent Tablets Orange Flavour

Do NOT take Berocca Effervescent Tablets Orange Flavour if you:

  • Have a known allergy or hypersensitivity to any ingredient in the product.
  • Have severe kidney impairment (GFR less than 30 ml/min), including individuals on dialysis.
  • Have kidney stones (nephrolithiasis) or a history of kidney stones.
  • Have severe hypercalcemia (abnormally high levels of calcium in the blood) or hypercalciuria (abnormally high calcium in the urine).
  • Have hemochromatosis (iron overload condition).

Pregnancy & Breastfeeding Warnings

  • Pregnancy and Breastfeeding: Consult your doctor. While most ingredients are safe in standard doses, it is recommended to consult a healthcare professional before taking this high-dose supplement during pregnancy or lactation.

Side Effects of Berocca Effervescent Tablets Orange Flavour

Side effects are generally mild and related to the digestive system or the high concentration of B-Vitamins.

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Mild gastrointestinal disturbances, such as constipation, diarrhoea, nausea, or stomach upset. A harmless yellow discolouration of the urine (due to Vitamin B2 - Riboflavin) is normal and expected.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (that may require medical attention):
    • Allergic Reactions: Rash, hives, swelling (especially of the face/tongue/throat), or difficulty breathing.
    • Specific Toxicity: Chronic overdose of Vitamin C or Vitamin B6 can lead to specific toxicities (e.g., nerve problems with B6.

What to do if side effects occur:

  • If stomach upset occurs, try taking the tablet with a meal.
  • If you experience any severe or persistent adverse effects, or signs of a serious allergic reaction, discontinue use immediately and consult a physician.

Precautions and Warnings of Berocca Effervescent Tablets Orange Flavour

  • Dosage Limit: Do NOT exceed the recommended daily dose of one tablet per day to avoid potential overdose of vitamins, particularly B6 and Vitamin C.
  • Excipients: The tablets contain sodium (salt), which must be taken into consideration by patients on a controlled sodium diet.
  • Lab Tests: High doses of Vitamin C may interfere with certain lab tests. Inform your physician when taking this product and planning diagnostic measures.
  • Drug Interactions: Consult your doctor or pharmacist if taking any other vitamins, minerals, or prescription medications, especially Levodopa (for Parkinson's disease) or certain antibiotics.
  • Dissolution: Ensure the tablet is fully dissolved in water before drinking.

searchable.name: Atadyn Syrup
searchable.brief_info: Atadyn Syrup is used to relieve symptoms of seasonal allergy (seasonal allergic rhinitis) and other upper respiratory tract allergies. This medicine works by restricting the action of a natural chemical (called as, histamine) which acts on the body cell sites (H1 histamine receptor). Atadyn Syrup is also used to treat long-term hives.
searchable.description:
Atadyn is used for the symptomatic relief of allergic conditions such as sneezing, rhinorrhea and itching, and ocular itching and burning. Also indicated for the relief of chronic urticaria and other allergic dermatological disorders.

Atadyn syrup relieve symptoms of seasonal allergy (seasonal allergic rhinitis) and other upper respiratory tract allergies. This medicine works by restricting the action of a natural chemical (called as, histamine) which acts on the body cell sites (H1 histamine receptor).Atadyn Syrupis also used to treat long-term hives.
Antihistamines
Atadyn belongs to the Antihistamines class of medicines. Antihistamines help in relieving allergic symptoms. They do so by blocking the effects of histamines. Histamines are produced by the body and in-turn cause fever, itching, sneezing, a runny nose, or watery eyes in allergic conditions.

Active ingredient: Loratidine

Atadyn Side-effects
fatigue
headache
nervousness
searchable.how_to_use: The typical adult dose of Atadyn is 10 mg once daily or 2 teaspoonfuls (10 mg) of syrup once daily. The typical dose for children is 5 mg (1 teaspoonful) once daily. The maximum dose for adults of Atadyn is 10 mg per day. It takes 1 to 3 hours for this medicine to start its action.
searchable.famasi_advice:
This medicine should not be used in patients with known allergic reaction to it. These patients are at an increased risk of severe allergic reaction. These patients may have the following symptoms if they use this medicine:
severe itching
red or purple coloured spots on the skin
hives
rash
photosensitivity reaction
swelling due to the fluid accumulation
severe allergic reaction (anaphylaxis)

searchable.name: Calpol Infant Oral Suspension 100ML
searchable.brief_info: Calpol Infant Oral Suspension is the essential, most widely used liquid medicine formulated to safely and effectively relieve pain and reduce fever in babies and young children, starting from 2 months of age. The 100ML bottle provides a convenient supply for frequent use during common childhood ailments.
searchable.description: Calpol Infant Oral Suspension (100ML) is a pleasant-tasting oral solution containing Paracetamol as its active ingredient (120 mg per 5 ml). Its formulation is specifically designed for the sensitive digestive systems of infants and young children, providing rapid and reliable relief from common childhood discomforts. Calpol Infant Suspension effectively lowers high temperatures and eases various types of mild to moderate pain, helping the child feel more comfortable and promoting rest. It is often the first line of treatment recommended by pediatricians and pharmacists for managing pain and fever in this age group.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Calpol Infant Oral Suspension

This is an Over-The-Counter (OTC) medicine. The dose is strictly determined by the child's age or weight.

  • Babies 2 - 3 months: A single 2.5 ml dose (only if fever is due to immunisation). A second 2.5 ml dose may be given 4 to 6 hours later, if necessary. If the fever is not due to immunisation, consult a doctor.
  • Infants 3 - 6 months: Give 2.5 ml.
  • Children 6 - 24 months: Give 5 ml.
  • Children 2 - 4 years: Give 7.5 ml (one and a half spoons/syringes).
  • Children 4 - 6 years: Give 10 ml (two spoons/syringes).
  • Do not exceed four doses in any 24 hours.

Proper Administration

  • Administration: For oral use only. Always use the syringe or measuring spoon supplied with the medicine to ensure the dose is accurately measured.
  • Frequency: For children over 3 months, repeat the dose every 4 to 6 hours as necessary. DO NOT give more than four doses in 24 hours.
  • Shaking: Shake the bottle thoroughly for at least 10 seconds before measuring the dose.

Duration: Do not give this medicine for more than 3 days without seeking medical advice.
searchable.specification:
• Pack Size: 100 ML Bottle
  • Form: Oral Suspension
  • Active Ingredient: Paracetamol 120 mg per 5 ml
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Calpol Infant Oral Suspension

  • Known Allergies: Do NOT give if your child has a known allergy to Paracetamol or any other ingredient in the medicine.
  • Severe Liver Disease: Do NOT give if the child has severe liver disease.

Side Effects of Calpol Infant Oral Suspension

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Rare): Allergic reactions (rash, itching), very rarely blood disorders.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (That May Require Medical Attention): Seek medical help immediately if your child takes too much, even if they appear well, due to the risk of delayed, severe liver damage.

Precautions and Warnings of Calpol Infant Oral Suspension

  • Overdose Risk: Accidental overdose is the primary risk and can be fatal. Adhere strictly to the maximum daily dose and interval.
  • Weight-Based Dosing: If the child is small or large for their age, consult a pharmacist or doctor who may recommend a dose based on their actual weight.

Fructose Intolerance: The medicine contains sweeteners (e.g., Sorbitol, Maltitol); children with hereditary fructose intolerance must NOT take this medicine.

  • Pack Size: 100 ML Bottle
  • Form: Oral Suspension

Active Ingredient: Paracetamol 120 mg per 5 ml

searchable.name: Strepsils Lozenges Honey and Lemon Flavour
searchable.brief_info: Strepsils Lozenges Honey & Lemon Flavour delivers soothing comfort and effective relief from the earliest signs of a sore throat with
searchable.description: Strepsils Lozenges Honey & Lemon Flavour is a trusted Over-The-Counter (OTC) medicine delivers a dual antiseptic action directly to the site of infection in the mouth and throat. The lozenges contain two powerful active ingredients that target the bacteria and microbes associated with throat discomfort. Enhanced with the classic, soothing flavour of natural Honey and zesty Lemon, Strepsils Lozenges not only fight the cause of infection but also lubricate and soothe the dry, painful area. It’s an essential, fast-acting treatment for alleviating pain and irritation.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Strepsils Lozenges Honey & Lemon Flavour

This is an Over-The-Counter (OTC) medicine.

  • Adults and Children over 6 years old: Dissolve one lozenge slowly in the mouth every 2 to 3 hours.
  • Maximum Dosage: Do not exceed a maximum of 12 lozenges in 24 hours.

Proper Administration

  • Administration: Allow the lozenge to dissolve completely in the mouth. This maximizes contact time with the throat and mouth tissue.
  • Best Time to Take: Use when the throat pain or irritation is most severe. Ensure consistent use throughout the day during waking hours.
  • Missed Dose Guidance: Do not take a double dose. Continue to use the medicine according to the recommended schedule.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Dosage of Strepsils Lozenges Honey & Lemon Flavour

This is an Over-The-Counter (OTC) medicine.

  • Adults and Children over 6 years old: Dissolve one lozenge slowly in the mouth every 2 to 3 hours.
  • Maximum Dosage: Do not exceed a maximum of 12 lozenges in 24 hours.

Proper Administration

  • Administration: Allow the lozenge to dissolve completely in the mouth. This maximizes contact time with the throat and mouth tissue.Contraindications of Strepsils Lozenges Honey & Lemon Flavour
  • Known Allergies: Do NOT take if you are allergic (hypersensitive) to 2,4-dichlorobenzyl alcohol, amylmetacresol, or any of the inactive ingredients, including honey or flavouring components.
  • Age Restriction: Not suitable for children under 6 years old due to the risk of choking on the lozenge.
  • Sugar Intolerance: Patients with rare hereditary problems of fructose intolerance, glucose-galactose malabsorption, or sucrose-isomaltose insufficiency should consult a doctor, as these lozenges often contain glucose and sucrose.

Side Effects of Strepsils Lozenges Honey & Lemon Flavour

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): None are typically common. Mild local irritation or temporary alteration of taste perception may occur.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (That May Require Medical Attention): Signs of severe allergic reaction (rash, swelling of the mouth/face, difficulty breathing). Report persistent, severe soreness or oral pain. If any severe symptoms occur, stop use immediately and consult a doctor.

Precautions and Warnings of Strepsils Lozenges Honey & Lemon Flavour


  • Best Time to Take: Use when the throat pain or irritation is most severe. Ensure consistent use throughout the day during waking hours.
  • Missed Dose Guidance: Do not take a double dose. Continue to use the medicine according to the recommended schedule.

searchable.name: Dioralyte Blackcurrent
searchable.brief_info: Dioralyte Blackcurrant is a clinically proven oral rehydration solution trusted for the rapid and effective replacement of essential fluids and body salts lost due to acute diarrhoea and vomiting.
searchable.description: Dioralyte Blackcurrant Sachets contain a standardized, glucose-based Oral Rehydration Salt (ORS) formulation that complies with World Health Organization (WHO) and ESPGHAN guidelines for fluid and electrolyte replacement. The solution's efficacy relies on the Glucose/Sodium Co-transport Mechanism, which ensures that the presence of glucose allows the rapid and effective absorption of sodium, and consequently, water across the intestinal wall. The pleasant blackcurrant flavour greatly improves patient compliance, which is absolutely vital for preventing dangerous dehydration, especially in children and the elderly, during episodes of acute fluid loss.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Dioralyte Blackcurrant Sachets

Dioralyte Blackcurrant Sachets is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) medicine.

Adults and Children (over 1 year): One sachet after each loose motion (watery stool), up to a maximum of 5 sachets per day for 3-4 days.

Proper Administration

  • Route: Oral powder for solution.
  • How to Prepare:
    1. Empty the contents of one sachet into a large glass.
    2. Add 200 ml of fresh drinking water (for infants, use freshly boiled and cooled water).
    3. Stir well until the powder is fully dissolved. Drink the entire glassful immediately.

Timing: Administer immediately after each episode of loose stool or vomiting. If vomiting is present, give small sips every 5 minutes and gradually increase.

Missed Dose Guidance

  • If a dose is missed, take the next sachet when you have the next loose stool. Do not take a double dose.
searchable.specification: The active ingredients are Dextrose, Sodium Chloride, Potassium Chloride, and Sodium Citrate/Bicarbonate. It is supplied as a blackcurrant-flavoured powder for oral solution, typically in a pack of 6 or 20 sachets. It is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) Oral Rehydration Therapy.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Dioralyte Blackcurrant Sachets

Do NOT take Dioralyte Blackcurrant Sachets if you:

  • Have a known allergy or hypersensitivity to any of the active substances or excipients (e.g., Aspartame, if present).
  • Have conditions causing glucose malabsorption.
  • Have intestinal obstruction or severe kidney failure (renal failure), as this can interfere with electrolyte balance.
  • Are experiencing severe or continuous vomiting where oral rehydration is impossible.

Pregnancy & Breastfeeding Warnings

  • Pregnancy and Breastfeeding: Consult your doctor. Dioralyte is generally considered safe and is essential for rehydration during pregnancy and breastfeeding. However, it should only be used on medical advice, especially concerning the sodium/potassium content.

Side Effects of Dioralyte Blackcurrant Sachets

Side effects are rare when the solution is prepared correctly.

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): None typically expected.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (that may require medical attention):
    • Electrolyte Imbalance: Can occur if the solution is prepared using too little or too much water (making it too strong or too weak), potentially leading to fluid imbalance.
    • Severe Allergic Reactions: Rash, hives, swelling of the lips, face, throat, or tongue, or difficulty breathing.

What to do if side effects occur:

  • If symptoms persist beyond 36 hours, or if you observe signs of a severe allergic reaction, stop the medication immediately and seek emergency medical attention.
  • If vomiting occurs, give the solution in smaller, more frequent sips.

Precautions and Warnings of Dioralyte Blackcurrant Sachets

  • Preparation is CRUCIAL: Always dissolve the contents of one sachet in exactly 200 ml of fresh drinking water. Using too little water makes the solution too strong (hypertonic), which can worsen dehydration or upset the balance of body salts.
  • Only Water: Do NOT dissolve the powder in milk, juice, soft drinks, or anything other than plain water.
  • Infants: For infants under 1 year, the product should only be given under medical supervision. Infants under 2 years with diarrhoea should be seen by a doctor as soon as possible.
  • Storage after Mixing: The liquid should be made up immediately before use. Any solution not used after 1 hour (or 24 hours if stored in a refrigerator) should be discarded.
  • Underlying Conditions: Consult a doctor if you are on a controlled sodium or potassium diet, or if you have severe kidney, liver, or heart problems.

searchable.name: Canesten Thrush Cream 20g
searchable.brief_info: For the treatment and soothing relief of external thrush symptoms.
searchable.description:
How do I use Canesten thrush external cream?

?? Women should apply Canesten thrush cream thinly to the vulva and surrounding area two to three times a day. Rub it in gently and then wash your hands. Women can keep using this cream until the symptoms of the infection have cleared up. However, you should consult your doctor if your symptoms have not got better within a week of using another medicine to treat the internal vaginal infection.

?? Men with symptoms of thrush should apply Canesten thrush cream thinly to the penis two to three times a day for up to two weeks. Rub the cream in gently and then wash your hands. Men should keep using this cream until the symptoms of the infection have cleared up. However, you should consult your doctor if your symptoms have not got better within two weeks of using Canesten thrush cream.

searchable.name: Durex Extra Safe Condom
searchable.brief_info: Durex Extra Safe Condoms are designed to provide maximum peace of mind during intercourse through the use of slightly thicker latex and extra lubrication. They are a reliable barrier method for preventing pregnancy and STIs.
searchable.description: Durex Extra Safe Condoms are standard-fit condoms made from transparent natural rubber latex but manufactured with a marginally thicker material than standard Durex condoms. They also feature extra silicone-based lubricant. This combination provides a greater sense of security regarding breakage and micro-tears, making them a preferred choice for individuals seeking added reassurance without compromising safety.
searchable.how_to_use:
Administration

  • Single Use: Use a new condom for every act of intercourse. Do not reuse.
  • Application: Place the condom on the tip of the erect penis before any sexual contact. Pinch the reservoir tip to remove air and roll it down to the base.
  • Withdrawal: Immediately after ejaculation, hold the rim of the condom at the base of the penis and withdraw while the penis is still erect.
searchable.specification:
• Pack Size: Varies (e.g., 3, 12, or bulk packs)
  • Form: Barrier Device (Thicker Latex, Extra Lubricated, Nominal Width 56 mm)
  • Material: Natural Rubber Latex
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications

  • Latex Allergy: Do NOT use if you or your partner has a known allergy to natural rubber latex. Consider a non-latex (polyisoprene or polyurethane) condom alternative if an allergy is present.

Side Effects

  • Common Side Effects (Rare and Local): Mild local irritation or slight warmth from the latex or lubricant.

Precautions and Warnings

  • Breakage Risk: No contraceptive method is 100% effective. If the condom breaks or slips off, consult a healthcare provider immediately regarding emergency contraception and STI testing.
  • Correct Use: Misuse dramatically increases the risk of failure.
  • Medications: Some vaginal medications may contain oil-based components. Check compatibility with your pharmacist before use.

searchable.name: Buttercup Original Cough Syrup 200ml
searchable.brief_info: Buttercup Original Cough Syrup is a classic, non-drowsy, oral liquid traditionally used for the symptomatic relief of coughs, hoarseness, and sore throats. Its formula is based on traditional medicinal plant ingredients known for their soothing properties.
searchable.description: Buttercup Original Cough Syrup (200ml) is a traditional herbal medicinal product designed to soothe the throat and provide temporary relief from irritating coughs. The syrup works by coating the throat and airways, reducing the irritation that triggers the cough reflex. It is a time-tested remedy, utilizing traditional plant extracts to offer gentle yet effective relief from dry, tickly, and spasmodic coughs, as well as associated hoarseness and sore throat discomfort. Its non-drowsy formulation makes it suitable for use throughout the day.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Buttercup Original Cough Syrup

This is an Over-The-Counter (OTC) medicine.

  • Adults and Children aged 12 years and over: Take 10 ml (two 5 ml spoonfuls).
  • Children aged 6 to 11 years: Take 5 ml (one 5 ml spoonful).
  • Do not exceed four doses in any 24 hours.

Proper Administration

  • Administration: For oral use only. Measure the dose precisely using a 5 ml medicine spoon or the measuring cup provided.
  • Frequency: Repeat the dose every 4 hours as needed.
  • Duration: Do not use continuously for more than 5 days without consulting a doctor, particularly if symptoms worsen or do not improve.
  • Children: Do not give to children under 6 years of age except on the advice of a physician.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Buttercup Original Cough Syrup

  • Known Allergies: Do NOT take if you have a known allergy to any of the plant extracts (e.g., squill, ipecacuanha) or any other ingredients.
  • Serious Respiratory Conditions: Do NOT use if you have severe respiratory failure, acute asthma, or conditions where cough suppression is inappropriate (e.g., a heavily productive or chesty cough where mucus clearance is vital).

Side Effects of Buttercup Original Cough Syrup

  • Common Side Effects (Mild): Nausea, vomiting, or stomach upset (due to Ipecacuanha's action, especially in higher doses).
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (Medical Attention Needed): Signs of an allergic reaction (rash, itching, swelling of the face or throat).

Precautions and Warnings of Buttercup Original Cough Syrup

  • Persistent Cough: If the cough persists for more than a week, or if it is accompanied by high fever, rash, or persistent headache, consult a doctor immediately.
  • Children's Safety: Keep all medicines, especially sweet-tasting syrups, out of the reach and sight of children.

Pregnancy & Breastfeeding: Consult your doctor or pharmacist before taking this product if you are pregnant or breastfeeding.

searchable.description:

Side Effects

searchable.how_to_use:

1 - 6 years: Each children sachet containing Artesunate 50mg and Amodiaquine base 150mg should be dissolved in 30ml clean water and taken daily for 3 days.

searchable.name: Moko Iodine Tincture
searchable.brief_info: Moko Iodine Tincture is a reliable, fast-acting external antiseptic trusted for its powerful ability to kill bacteria and prevent infection in minor cuts, scrapes, and burns.
searchable.description: Moko Iodine Tincture is a dark brown, concentrated solution of elemental Iodine and Potassium Iodide (or Sodium Iodide) dissolved in a mixture of water and a high percentage of Alcohol (Ethanol). This formulation is classified as a "weak iodine solution" and is known for its powerful bactericidal, fungicidal, and virucidal action. The alcohol component allows the iodine to quickly penetrate the skin and enhances the antiseptic effect, while the iodide salts increase the solubility of the elemental iodine. Moko Iodine Tincture is an indispensable item for first aid kits in the home, clinic, and workplace, providing robust protection against common wound contamination.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Moko Iodine Tincture

Moko Iodine Tincture is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) antiseptic.

Apply a small amount to the cut, scrape, or burn one to three times daily, as needed.

Proper Administration

  • Route: Topical liquid.
  • How to Use:
    1. Clean the affected area gently.
    2. Using a clean cotton swab or cotton wool, apply a small amount of the tincture directly to the minor wound or scrape.
    3. Allow the area to dry completely before covering with a sterile bandage, if necessary.
  • Timing: Apply 1 to 3 times daily, as needed.

Missed Dose Guidance

  • If you forget to apply, apply it as soon as possible. Continue with the maximum frequency of three times a day as needed.
searchable.specification: The active ingredients are Iodine (typically 2.0% to 2.5% w/v) and Potassium Iodide (typically 2.5% w/v) in an Ethanol/Water base. It is supplied as a dark brown topical liquid (common sizes are 15 mL or 25 mL). It is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) First Aid Antiseptic.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Moko Iodine Tincture

Do NOT use Moko Iodine Tincture if you:

  • Have a known allergy or hypersensitivity to iodine, iodide salts, or any other component (e.g., alcohol).
  • Have a thyroid problem (e.g., hyperthyroidism), as prolonged or extensive use may interfere with thyroid function.
  • Are treating newborn infants (may cause skin and thyroid problems).
  • Are applying it over large areas of the skin or on deep, puncture wounds, animal bites, or serious burns.

Pregnancy & Breastfeeding Warnings

  • Pregnancy and Breastfeeding: Consult your doctor. Iodine should not be used in pregnancy or breastfeeding unless specifically advised by a doctor, as excessive iodine intake may cause thyroid problems in the baby.

Side Effects of Moko Iodine Tincture

Side effects are generally localized to the application site.

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Temporary brown staining of the skin (which fades over time) and clothing. Mild skin irritation, dryness, itching, or redness at the site of application (often due to the alcohol content).
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (that may require medical attention):
    • Severe Allergic Reactions: Hives, swelling in the face or hands, chest tightness, or trouble breathing.
    • Thyroid Dysfunction: With long-term or widespread use, it may disrupt thyroid function.

What to do if side effects occur:

  • For mild skin irritation, ensure you are not covering the area with a tight bandage. If irritation persists, discontinue use and consult a healthcare provider.
  • If swallowed: Seek immediate emergency assistance or call a poison helpline. Do not induce vomiting. Give milk or a solution of cornstarch and water, and transport to a hospital immediately.
  • Eye Contact: Flush the eye immediately and thoroughly with large amounts of water and seek medical assistance.

Precautions and Warnings of Moko Iodine Tincture

  • Poisonous/External Use Only: Iodine Tincture is poisonous if swallowed. It is strictly for external use on the skin only. Do not swallow or use in the eyes.
  • Flammable: Due to the high alcohol content, the solution is highly flammable. Keep it away from heat, flames, or sparks.
  • Duration of Use: Do not use for longer than one week unless directed by a doctor. Prolonged use can increase the risk of side effects.
  • Avoid Tight Dressings: Do not cover the wound with a tight dressing or bandage, as this may increase the chance of side effects.
  • Drug Interactions: Do not apply other medicines or cosmetics to skin treated with iodine tincture unless advised by a healthcare provider.

searchable.name: Flucor day Capsule
searchable.brief_info: Flucor Day Capsule is a specially formulated multi-action medication designed to provide rapid relief from the symptoms of the common cold, flu, and nasal congestion without causing daytime sleepiness. By combining a potent pain reliever with a powerful decongestant, Flucor Day helps you remain productive and alert while your body fights off the infection.
searchable.description: Flucor Day is engineered for daytime use. Unlike many "night" formulas that contain sedating antihistamines, this capsule focuses on clearing the airways and reducing body aches. It works by narrowing the swollen blood vessels in the nasal passages and elevating the body's overall pain threshold, making it easier to breathe and move throughout the day.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Flucor Day
  • Adults and Children over 12 years: Usually 1 to 2 capsules taken every 4 to 6 hours as needed.
  • Maximum Daily Dose: Do NOT exceed 8 capsules in a 24-hour period.
  • Note: Ensure you do not take this medication within 4 hours of a "Night" cold formula to avoid duplicating ingredients.

Proper Administration
  • Method: Swallow the capsule whole with a full glass of water.
  • Food: Can be taken with or without food. If you have a sensitive stomach, taking it with a small snack is recommended.
  • Timing: As this is a "Day" formula, it is best taken in the morning and afternoon. Taking it very late in the evening may interfere with sleep in some individuals due to the decongestant effect.

How to Store and Dispose of Flucor Day
  • Storage: Store in a cool, dry place below 30°C. Keep away from direct sunlight and moisture.
  • Disposal: Keep out of reach of children. Return any unused or expired capsules to your local pharmacy.

Quick Tips for Flucor Day
  • Read the Label: Many other cold medications contain Paracetamol. Always check labels to ensure you aren't "double-dosing" on Paracetamol, which can harm the liver.
  • Steam Inhalation: For even faster relief of congestion, use Flucor Day alongside steam inhalation (hot water with a drop of eucalyptus oil).
  • Stay Hydrated: Drink plenty of water to help thin the mucus in your sinuses while the medication works.
searchable.specification:
• Pack Size: Usually 10 or 20 capsules per blister pack.
  • Form: Oral Capsule.
  • Active Ingredients: Paracetamol 500mg, Pseudoephedrine HCl 30mg.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Safety and Precautions

Contraindications of Flucor Day
  • Hypertension (High Blood Pressure): Decongestants like Pseudoephedrine can raise blood pressure; consult your doctor if you have heart issues.
  • MAOIs: Do NOT use if you have taken a Monoamine Oxidase Inhibitor (antidepressant) in the last 14 days.
  • Severe Kidney/Liver Disease: Use with extreme caution.
  • Allergy: Do not use if you are allergic to Paracetamol or Pseudoephedrine.

Side Effects of Flucor Day
  • Common: Dry mouth, nervousness, or difficulty sleeping (insomnia).
  • Less Common: Rapid heartbeat (palpitations), dizziness, or mild nausea.
  • Action: If you experience chest pain, severe headache, or a fast, pounding heartbeat, stop use immediately and seek medical attention.

Precautions and Warnings
  • Pregnancy & Breastfeeding: Consult a healthcare provider before use. Pseudoephedrine is generally avoided in the first trimester.
  • Glaucoma & Thyroid: Use with caution if you have glaucoma or an overactive thyroid gland.
  • Diabetes: Decongestants may occasionally affect blood sugar levels.

searchable.name: Neofylin Syrup
searchable.brief_info: Neofylin cough syrup is used for Cough relief, Common cold, Allergy, Hay fever, Watery eyes, Itchy throat/skin, Anaphylactic shock, Rhinitis, Urticaria, Patients with metabolic alkalosis and other conditions.
searchable.description: Neofylin Syrup is a specialized bronchodilator and respiratory treatment formulated to ease breathing for individuals suffering from obstructive airway diseases. By combining a potent xanthine derivative with an expectorant, Neofylin helps to widen the air passages in the lungs and thin out stubborn mucus. It is a trusted choice for managing chronic respiratory conditions, providing both symptomatic relief and improved lung function for adults and children alike.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Neofylin Syrup This is a Prescription-Only Medicine (POM). Take as directed by the pharmacist or physician.

  • Administration: Use a calibrated measuring cup or spoon for accurate dosing.
  • Timing: Usually taken 2 to 3 times daily. Taking it at the same time each day helps maintain steady levels in the blood.
  • Food: Can be taken with food if it causes stomach upset.
  • Missed Dose: Take as soon as you remember. Skip it if the next dose is less than 4 hours away. Never double the dose.

How to Store and Dispose

  • Storage: Store in a cool, dry place below 30°C. Keep the bottle tightly closed and away from direct sunlight.
  • Disposal: Keep out of reach of children. Return unused syrup to a pharmacy for safe disposal.

Quick Tips for Neofylin Syrup

  • Hydration: Drink plenty of water while taking this syrup to help Guaifenesin thin the mucus effectively.
  • Consistency: Do not skip doses, even if you feel you are breathing better.
  • Avoid Triggers: Stay away from dust, smoke, and allergens while on treatment to speed up recovery.
  • Monitor Sleep: If the syrup causes insomnia, ask your doctor about adjusting the timing of your evening dose.
searchable.specification:
• Volume: Typically 100ml or 125ml bottles.
  • Form: Oral Liquid Syrup.
  • Category: Bronchodilator / Expectorant.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Neofylin Syrup

  • Hypersensitivity: Do not use if you are allergic to Theophylline, Caffeine, or Guaifenesin.
  • Acute Heart Conditions: Not suitable for patients with recent myocardial infarction or acute tachyarrhythmias.
  • Peptic Ulcers: Use is contraindicated in patients with active gastric or duodenal ulcers.
  • Age: Consult a doctor before administering to infants or very young children.

Side Effects of Neofylin Syrup

  • Common: Nausea, vomiting, headache, restlessness, and insomnia (due to the stimulant nature of theophylline).
  • Serious: Rapid or irregular heartbeat (palpitations), tremors, or seizures.
  • Action: If you experience severe vomiting or a persistent racing heart, stop the medication and seek medical attention immediately.

Precautions and Warnings

  • Caffeine Intake: Limit consumption of coffee, tea, and chocolate, as these can increase the side effects of Theophylline.
  • Smoking: Tobacco smoking can change how the body processes this medication; inform your doctor if you smoke.
  • Pregnancy & Breastfeeding: Use only if clearly needed and prescribed by a physician.

searchable.name: Night Nurse Capsules
searchable.brief_info: Night Nurse Capsules are a trusted formula designed to provide unbeatable, multi-symptom relief from the major discomforts of colds, chills, and flu, ensuring you get the peaceful, restful sleep vital for recovery.
searchable.description: Night Nurse Capsules are a powerful combination medication formulated specifically for night-time use. It contains a triple-action blend of a painkiller and fever reducer, a sedating antihistamine, and a cough suppressant. The capsules target the symptoms that most commonly disrupt sleep during illness, such as headaches, aches, fever, runny nose, and dry, tickly coughs. By effectively controlling these symptoms and actively inducing drowsiness, Night Nurse Capsules clear the airways and soothe the body, helping you fall asleep faster and ensuring a deep, restorative night of sleep crucial for immune function and recovery.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Night Nurse Capsules

Night Nurse Capsules is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) medicine.

Take 2 capsules just before bedtime with a drink of water. Only take one dose per night.

Proper Administration

  • Route: Oral capsule.
  • How to Take: Swallow the two capsules whole with a drink of water (milk is not recommended for Promethazine absorption).
  • Timing: Take the single dose just before bedtime (about 20 minutes before you plan to get into bed).
  • Maximum Dose: Do NOT exceed 2 capsules per night. Only take one dose in any 24-hour period.

Missed Dose Guidance

  • This is a single, nighttime dose. If you forget to take it and it is still early in the night, take the two capsules. If it is nearly morning, skip the dose to avoid next-day drowsiness, and resume the following night. Do not take a double dose.
searchable.specification: The active ingredients are Paracetamol 500 mg, Promethazine Hydrochloride 10 mg, and Dextromethorphan Hydrobromide 7.5 mg per capsule. The typical dose is 2 capsules. It is supplied as oral capsules, often in packs of 10 or 20. It is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) Cold and Flu Remedy (Night-Time/Sedating).
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Night Nurse Capsules

Do NOT take Night Nurse Capsules if you:

  • Have a known allergy or hypersensitivity to Paracetamol, Promethazine, Dextromethorphan, or any other ingredient.
  • Have severe liver or kidney problems.
  • Have a chest infection, worsening asthma, or severe respiratory problems (as the sedative can worsen breathing).
  • Are taking or have taken Monoamine Oxidase Inhibitors (MAOIs) prescribed for depression in the last two weeks.
  • Are children under 16 years of age.

Pregnancy & Breastfeeding Warnings

  • Pregnancy: Do NOT take Night Nurse Capsules if you are pregnant. While Paracetamol is generally considered safe, there is insufficient data on the safety of Promethazine and Dextromethorphan during pregnancy. Consult your doctor.
  • Breastfeeding: Do NOT take Night Nurse Capsules if you are breastfeeding. The active ingredients can pass into breast milk and cause sedation or other adverse effects in the infant. Consult your doctor.

Side Effects of Night Nurse Capsules

Side effects are common due to the sedating antihistamine component.

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Drowsiness (very common and intended), dizziness, blurred vision, dry mouth, difficulty concentrating, unsteadiness, headache, constipation, or difficulty passing urine.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (that may require medical attention):
    • Serotonin Syndrome: Symptoms like confusion, restlessness, sweating, shivering, sudden jerks of muscles, or increased heart rate (risk increases if taken with certain antidepressants).
    • Severe Allergic Reactions: Skin rash, peeling, swelling of the mouth/face, or difficulty breathing.
    • Liver Damage: Symptoms of unexplained bruising, bleeding, nausea, vomiting, or yellowing of the eyes/skin.

What to do if side effects occur:

  • Due to the Promethazine, drowsiness the next morning is possible; do not drive or operate machinery until you are certain the effects have fully worn off.
  • If you experience any severe symptoms, signs of liver damage, or a severe allergic reaction, stop the medication immediately and seek emergency medical attention.

Precautions and Warnings of Night Nurse Capsules

  • Paracetamol Overdose: CRITICAL WARNING: Do not take any other product containing Paracetamol within four hours of taking Night Nurse Capsules, and do not exceed the maximum daily dose of 4000 mg of Paracetamol from all sources. Taking too much Paracetamol can cause delayed, serious liver damage.
  • Alcohol: Do NOT drink alcohol while using Night Nurse Capsules, as alcohol will dangerously increase the sedative and dizzying effects.
  • Duration of Use: Do not take for more than 3 consecutive nights without medical advice. Prolonged use may lead to dependence.
  • Drug Interactions: Consult your doctor or pharmacist if you are taking antidepressants (especially MAOIs or SSRIs), medicines for heart rhythm (e.g., Amiodarone), medicines for nausea (Metoclopramide/Domperidone), or blood thinners (Warfarin).

Driving: Do not drive or operate machinery if you feel drowsy, dizzy, or notice blurred vision.

searchable.name: Gestid Suspension 200ml
searchable.brief_info: Gestid Suspension is used for Stomach acid, Heartburn, Sour stomach, Peptic ulcer, Flatulence, Gastroesophageal reflux, Peptic ulcer pain, Kidney problems, Increases water in the intestines, Retinal detachment and other conditions.
searchable.description:

searchable.name: Rhinathiol Expectorant Children Syrup
searchable.brief_info: Rhinathiol Children Syrup contains Carbocisteine, a gentle mucolytic agent trusted for effectively thinning and loosening thick, sticky mucus, making it easier for children to clear chest congestion and breathe easily.
searchable.description: Rhinathiol Children Syrup 2% is a raspberry and cherry-flavored oral solution formulated specifically for children aged 2 years and older who are suffering from chesty, productive coughs. The syrup contains the active ingredient Carbocisteine, which works directly on the bronchial secretions (mucus). By making the mucus less sticky and viscous, Rhinathiol Children Syrup helps the child's natural coughing mechanism become more effective at expelling the accumulated phlegm from the chest and airways. This action provides relief from bronchial congestion, particularly during episodes of acute bronchitis, promoting easier breathing and improved comfort.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Rhinathiol Children Syrup 2%

Rhinathiol Children Syrup 2% is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) medicine (Non-Prescription in many markets).

Take as directed by the pharmacist or physician.

Typical Dosage Guidelines:

  • Children aged 2 to 5 years: One 5 mL measuring spoon (100 mg Carbocisteine) twice a day.
  • Children aged over 5 years: One 5 mL measuring spoon (100 mg Carbocisteine) three times a day.
  • Duration: Do not use for more than 8 to 10 days.

Proper Administration

  • Route: Oral liquid (syrup).
  • How to Take: Shake the bottle well before use. Measure the exact dose using the 5 mL measuring spoon or cup provided.
  • Timing: Administer at consistent intervals throughout the day. It can be taken with or without food.
  • Coughing Encouraged: While taking this medicine, encourage the child to make an effort to cough to help expel the thinned phlegm.

Missed Dose Guidance

  • If you miss a dose, give it as soon as you remember.
  • Do not give a double dose to make up for a forgotten dose.
searchable.specification: The active ingredient is Carbocisteine (2g/100mL or 100 mg/5 mL). It is supplied as a raspberry and cherry-flavored oral syrup in a 125 mL bottle. It is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) Mucolytic (Chesty Cough) Medicine for children 2 years and older.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Rhinathiol Children Syrup 2%

Do NOT give Rhinathiol Children Syrup 2% if the child:

  • Is under 2 years of age (due to the risk of worsening bronchial congestion).
  • Has a known allergy or hypersensitivity to Carbocisteine, methyl parahydroxybenzoate (E218), cochineal red A (E124), or any other ingredients in the syrup.
  • Has a history of active stomach or intestinal ulcers (gastroduodenal ulcers).
  • Is taking cough suppressant medications (medicines that suppress the cough reflex).

Pregnancy & Breastfeeding Warnings

  • General: This product is for children. If an adult uses this product, they should note: Consult your doctor. The use of carbocisteine is not recommended during pregnancy or breastfeeding due to limited data.

Side Effects of Rhinathiol Children Syrup 2%

Side effects are generally related to the digestive system or rare allergic reactions.

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Digestive disorders, such as stomach pain, nausea, vomiting, or diarrhoea. If these occur, the dose may need to be reduced.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (that may require medical attention):
    • Severe Allergic Reactions: Worsening of bronchial congestion (especially in infants), appearance of severe skin rashes, sometimes with blisters (erythema multiforme, Stevens-Johnson syndrome), swelling of the face/neck (angioedema), or difficulty breathing.
    • Digestive Bleeding: Although rare, stomach or intestinal bleeding has been reported.

What to do if side effects occur:

  • For mild stomach pain or diarrhoea, reduce the dose and ensure the child is taking it with food.
  • If you observe any sign of a severe skin rash (spots or blisters), or difficulty breathing, stop the medication immediately and seek emergency medical assistance.

Precautions and Warnings of Rhinathiol Children Syrup 2%

  • Age Limit: Strictly contraindicated in children under 2 years of age.
  • Persistent Cough: If symptoms worsen or persist after 8 to 10 days of treatment, consult your doctor to check for a persistent underlying condition.
  • Cough Suppressants: DO NOT combine this mucolytic syrup with cough suppressants (medicines for dry cough) or medicines that dry up bronchial secretions, as this may lead to a dangerous buildup of thinned mucus in the chest.
  • Diabetic Patients: The syrup contains sucrose (sugar). This must be taken into account for children with diabetes or those on a low-sugar diet.
  • Excipient Sensitivity: Contains ingredients that can cause allergic reactions.

searchable.name: Sirdalud 2mg
searchable.brief_info: Sirdalud is a potent, central-acting skeletal muscle relaxant containing Tizanidine. It is designed to provide rapid relief from painful, involuntary muscle contractions (spasms) and chronic muscle stiffness (spasticity). By targeting the source of the tension in the spinal cord, Sirdalud helps restore mobility and reduces discomfort associated with back pain, neck strain, and neurological conditions.
searchable.description: Sirdalud belongs to a class of medications known as alpha-2 adrenergic agonists. Unlike some relaxants that act directly on the muscle fibers, Sirdalud works within the central nervous system. It is particularly effective for managing acute muscle pain as well as chronic spasticity resulting from neurological disorders. Because it can be adjusted to individual needs, it is a versatile tool for both short-term injury recovery and long-term symptom management.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Sirdalud This is a Prescription-Only Medicine (POM). Take exactly as directed by your physician. Dosage must be individualized to find the balance between relief and side effects (like drowsiness).

  • For Painful Spasms: The usual dose is 2mg to 4mg taken three times daily.
  • For Spasticity: The starting dose is usually 2mg daily, gradually increased by 2mg increments at weekly intervals until the desired effect is reached.

Proper Administration

  • Method: Swallow the tablet whole with a glass of water.
  • Consistency: Sirdalud can be taken with or without food, but you should choose one way and be consistent, as food can change how much medicine your body absorbs.
  • Timing: If you experience significant drowsiness, your doctor may suggest taking the largest portion of your daily dose at bedtime.
  • Missed Dose: Take it as soon as you remember. If it is almost time for your next dose, skip the missed one. Never double the dose.

How to Store and Dispose of Sirdalud

  • Storage: Store in the original package in a cool, dry place below 30°C.
  • Disposal: Keep out of reach of children. Return unused or expired medication to a pharmacy for safe disposal.

Quick Tips for Sirdalud

  • Do Not Stop Abruptly: If you have been taking Sirdalud at high doses for a long time, stopping suddenly can cause a rapid heartbeat and high blood pressure. Always taper the dose under medical supervision.
  • Drowsiness Warning: This medication often causes significant sleepiness. Avoid driving or operating machinery until you are certain of your reaction.
  • Avoid Alcohol: Alcohol can dangerously increase the sedative effects of Sirdalud and should be avoided.
searchable.specification:
• Pack Size: Usually 30 tablets per pack.
  • Form: Oral Tablet (available in 2mg and 4mg strengths).
  • Active Ingredient: Tizanidine.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Safety and Precautions

Contraindications of Sirdalud

  • Liver Function: Do NOT use if you have severely impaired liver function.
  • Drug Interactions: Do NOT take Sirdalud if you are taking potent inhibitors of CYP1A2, such as Fluvoxamine or Ciprofloxacin (an antibiotic). Combining these can lead to dangerously high levels of Tizanidine.
  • Allergies: Avoid if you have a known hypersensitivity to Tizanidine.

Side Effects of Sirdalud

  • Common: Drowsiness, fatigue, dizziness, dry mouth, and a slight decrease in blood pressure.
  • Rare: Nausea, stomach upset, or a transient rise in liver enzymes.
  • Action: If you experience severe weakness, fainting, or yellowing of the skin/eyes (jaundice), stop use and see a doctor immediately.

Precautions and Warnings

  • Blood Pressure: It may cause low blood pressure (hypotension). Be careful when standing up quickly from a sitting or lying position.
  • Kidney Health: Patients with renal impairment may require a lower starting dose and frequent monitoring.
  • Pregnancy & Breastfeeding: Not recommended unless the benefit clearly outweighs the risk. Consult your healthcare provider.

searchable.name: Lonart Suspension60ml
searchable.brief_info: Lonart Suspension is used to treat acute and uncomplicated malarial infections in patients weighing 5 kg (11 lb) and above. It is a prescription medicine. This medicine works by killing the parasitic organisms that cause malaria by blocking the synthesis of nucleic acid and proteins. This medicine helps by preventing the growth of malarial parasites.
searchable.description:
How to Use: 

Lonart Suspension is eaten with food. Infants and children who are unable to swallow the tablets, Lonart tablets must be crushed and mixed with a small amount of water (one to two teaspoons). If the patient vomits within 1 to 2 hours of taking the Lonart, a repeated dose of Lonart should be used. On the other hand, if the patient vomits the repeated dose, the patient should follow another alternative antimalarial for treatment.

The typical dose of Lonart is 80 mg/480 mg as a single initial dose, repeated as 80 mg/480 mg after 8 hours in a day and then followed by four doses (80 mg/480 mg) twice daily for the following two days. The usual dose for children is 20 mg/120 mg as a single initial dose, repeated as 20 mg/120 mg after 8 hours in a day and then followed by one dose (20 mg/120 mg) twice daily for the following two days. The maximum adult dose of Lonart is 160 mg/960 mg in a day.

searchable.name: Otrivin Adult Nasal Drops
searchable.brief_info: Otrivin Adult Nasal Drops is a widely used Over-The-Counter (OTC) medication providing rapid and effective relief from nasal congestion. It is a potent vasoconstrictor designed for short-term use to clear blocked nasal passages quickly.
searchable.description: Otrivin Adult Nasal Drops contain the active ingredient Xylometazoline Hydrochloride (0.1%), which belongs to the class of sympathomimetic decongestants. The drops are formulated for direct application into the nasal passages. Xylometazoline works by causing the swollen blood vessels in the nasal lining to narrow (vasoconstriction), which quickly reduces swelling and excessive nasal secretions. This action typically starts working within minutes (2 to 5 minutes) and provides long-lasting relief (up to 12 hours), allowing users to breathe easily through the nose. Moisturizing ingredients like Sorbitol and Hypromellose are often included to prevent dryness and irritation.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Otrivin Adult Nasal Drops

This is an Over-The-Counter (OTC) medicine.

  • Adults and Adolescents (12 years and over): Instill 2 to 3 drops into each nostril, 2 to 3 times daily, as necessary.
  • Maximum Daily Dose: Do not exceed 3 applications in each nostril daily. Do not use more often than every 8 to 10 hours.

Proper Administration

  • Preparation: Blow your nose gently before application to clear any mucus.
  • Application: Tilt the head back, or lie down. Without touching the dropper to the nostril, apply the prescribed number of drops into each nostril. Keep the head tilted back for a short time to allow the drops to spread.
  • Hygiene: After each use, clean and dry the dropper before replacing it. Do not share the bottle to avoid spreading infection.

Duration: Do not use this product for more than 3 to 7 consecutive days without medical advice.
searchable.specification:
• Form: Nasal Drops (0.1% concentration)
  • Active Ingredient: Xylometazoline Hydrochloride (1 mg/mL)
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Otrivin Adult Nasal Drops

  • Known Allergies: Do NOT use if you are allergic to Xylometazoline or any other ingredients (e.g., Benzalkonium Chloride).
  • Specific Nasal Conditions: Do NOT use if you have narrow-angle glaucoma or inflammatory nasal dryness (rhinitis sicca or atrophic rhinitis).
  • Recent Surgery: Do NOT use after recent neurosurgery (trans-sphenoidal hypophysectomy or surgery exposing the dura mater).
  • MAOIs/Antidepressants: Do NOT use if you are taking or have taken a Monoamine Oxidase Inhibitor (MAOI) within the last two weeks, or if you are taking tri- and tetra-cyclic antidepressants, due to the risk of severe blood pressure increase.

Side Effects of Otrivin Adult Nasal Drops

  • Common Side Effects (Local): Local burning sensation, stinging, dry nose, nasal discomfort, headache, or nausea.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (That May Require Medical Attention): Signs of too much medicine being absorbed (dizziness, pounding/fast heartbeat, high blood pressure), or a severe allergic reaction (difficulty breathing, swelling of the face/throat). Seek immediate medical attention if systemic symptoms occur.

Precautions and Warnings of Otrivin Adult Nasal Drops

  • Systemic Risk: Use with caution if you have underlying conditions such as high blood pressure (hypertension), heart disease (including long QT syndrome), diabetes mellitus, or an overactive thyroid gland (hyperthyroidism), as Xylometazoline is a sympathomimetic agent and can increase blood pressure and heart rate.
  • Pregnancy & Breastfeeding: Consult your doctor before use. Due to the potential for systemic vasoconstriction, use during pregnancy should be avoided unless strictly necessary.

Visual Disturbances: Rarely, transient visual impairment can occur; if you experience blurred vision, do not drive or operate machinery.
