searchable.name: Moxie Paracetamol Suspension for Kids
searchable.brief_info: Moxie Paracetamol Suspension for Kids is a pleasantly flavored oral suspension (liquid medicine) specifically formulated to provide rapid relief from pain and fever in babies and children aged 0 to 12 years.
searchable.description: Moxie Paracetamol Suspension for Kids containing the active ingredient Paracetamol (Acetaminophen) at a concentration of 120 mg/5 ml, this Over-the-Counter (OTC) product is a highly effective analgesic (pain reliever) and antipyretic (fever reducer). The liquid form, supplied with an accurate measuring device, ensures precise dosing and easy administration, which is vital for pediatric care.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Moxie Paracetamol Suspension for Kids

WARNING: Always base the dose on the child's age and weight, and never exceed the stated dose.

  • Administration: For oral use only. Shake the bottle for at least 10 seconds before use. Always use the measuring spoon or syringe supplied with the pack to ensure accuracy.
  • Best Time to Take It: Can be taken with or without food.
  • Dosing Interval: The usual interval is every 4 to 6 hours, as needed.
  • Maximum Dose: Do not give more than 4 doses in any 24-hour period.
  • Overdose Guidance: Immediate medical advice must be sought in the event of an overdose, even if the child appears well. This is due to the risk of delayed, serious liver damage.
searchable.specification: Pack Size: Moxie Paracetamol Suspension for Kids (120 mg/5 ml) is typically supplied in a 100 mL bottle.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Moxie Paracetamol Suspension for Kids

Do NOT give Moxie Paracetamol Suspension if the child:

  • Drug Allergies: Is allergic (hypersensitive) to Paracetamol or any of the inactive ingredients (e.g., colorants or preservatives).
  • Concurrent Medication: Is taking any other product that contains paracetamol.
  • Organ Impairment: Has severe liver problems or severe kidney problems.
  • Hereditary Intolerance: Has a rare genetic disorder like hereditary fructose intolerance (HFI), as the suspension may contain fructose-releasing sugars.

Side Effects of Moxie Paracetamol Suspension for Kids

Paracetamol is generally well-tolerated at the correct dose, but vigilance for severe reactions is essential.

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Generally rare, but may include mild stomach upset, nausea, or vomiting.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (Requires Immediate Medical Attention):
    • Allergic Reactions: Rash, hives, swelling of the face, lips, tongue, or difficulty breathing.
    • Blood and Liver Disorders: Pale skin, feeling unusually tired, unexplained bruising or bleeding, getting more infections than usual, or yellowing of the skin/eyes (jaundice).
    • Severe Skin Reactions: Reddening of the skin, blistering, or peeling (very rare but serious).
  • What to do if side effects occur: If you notice any signs of a serious allergic reaction or severe skin reaction, stop giving the medicine immediately and seek urgent medical help.

Precautions and Warnings of Moxie Paracetamol Suspension for Kids

  • Before Starting Moxie Paracetamol: Consult your doctor if the child has severe malnutrition, chronic alcoholism, or is taking the antibiotic Flucloxacillin, due to the risk of a serious blood and fluid abnormality (high anion gap metabolic acidosis).
  • During Use: Adhere strictly to the recommended interval (at least 4 hours) and do not give the medicine for more than 3 days without medical consultation.

Dosing Accuracy: Dosing should ideally be based on weight, but follow the age-based chart provided with the specific product, using the correct measuring device. Always seek a doctor's advice if the child is very small or large for their age.

searchable.name: Zetgel Emulgel
searchable.brief_info: Zetgel Emulgel is a powerful, triple-action topical analgesic and anti-inflammatory treatment designed for targeted relief of musculoskeletal and joint pain.
searchable.description: Zetgel Emulgel formulation combines the benefits of a moisturizing cream and a fast-acting gel, allowing the active ingredients to penetrate deep into the site of pain and inflammation. Zetgel Emulgel contains a potent Non-Steroidal Anti-Inflammatory Drug (NSAID) along with soothing counter-irritants to provide immediate cooling relief and long-lasting pain reduction. It is the ideal choice for individuals seeking quick, effective, localized relief from common aches, sprains, strains, and arthritic joint discomfort without the systemic side effects of oral painkillers.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Zetgel Emulgel

  • Dosage: Apply a small amount (about the size of a cherry or walnut, depending on the area) to the affected area.
  • Frequency: Gently massage the gel into the skin 3 to 4 times a day or as directed by a healthcare professional.

Proper Administration

  • Administration: Apply a thin layer of the Emulgel to the painful area and gently rub it in until the gel is fully absorbed into the skin.
  • Hand Washing: Wash your hands immediately after application unless the hands themselves are the area being treated.

Duration of Treatment: The duration depends on the indication; relief for sprains/strains is typically shorter (up to 2 weeks), while arthritis pain may require longer application (up to 3 weeks), but always follow doctor's advice for chronic use.
searchable.specification:
• Pack Size: Varies (Commonly 35g or 50g tube)
  • Form: Emulgel (Topical Gel)
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Zetgel Emulgel

  • Hypersensitivity: Do NOT use if you have a known allergy or hypersensitivity to Diclofenac, aspirin, or any other Non-Steroidal Anti-Inflammatory Drugs (NSAIDs), or any components of the gel (Menthol, Methyl Salicylate).
  • Asthma: It is contraindicated in patients who have experienced asthma, hives (urticaria), or acute rhinitis after taking aspirin or other NSAIDs (Aspirin-sensitive asthma).
  • Skin Integrity: Do not apply to cuts, abrasions, open wounds, infected lesions, or areas of active eczema or dermatitis.
  • Pregnancy & Breastfeeding Warnings: Safety for use in the third trimester of pregnancy is not recommended. Consult a doctor before using during pregnancy or while breastfeeding.
  • Age Restrictions: Not generally recommended for children under 12 years unless advised by a doctor.

Side Effects of Zetgel Emulgel

Side effects are mostly localized at the application site due to low systemic absorption.

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Localized skin reactions such as mild rash, itching, redness (erythema), dry skin, or a mild, transient burning sensation at the site of application (due to Menthol/Methyl Salicylate).
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (That May Require Medical Attention): Signs of a serious allergic reaction (swelling of the face/throat, wheezing, hives), or systemic effects like unusual or increased pain at the application site, nausea, or ringing in the ears. If severe side effects occur, stop use immediately and seek medical attention.

Precautions and Warnings of Zetgel Emulgel

  • External Use Only: This is for external application only. Do not take by mouth or allow it to contact eyes, nose, mouth, or mucous membranes. If contact occurs, rinse thoroughly with clean water.
  • Combination with Oral NSAIDs: Avoid concurrent use with oral NSAIDs (like ibuprofen or aspirin) unless directed by a physician, as this can increase the risk of systemic side effects.
  • Dressings and Heat: Do not apply tight or air-tight occlusive bandages over the treated area. Avoid applying heat (like heating pads) immediately before or after application, as this can increase absorption and risk of side effects.

Sun Sensitivity (Photosensitivity): Diclofenac can cause increased sun sensitivity. Avoid exposure of the treated area to direct sunlight or tanning beds during treatment.

searchable.name: Cyprodine Capsule
searchable.brief_info: Cyprodine is a potent medication containing Cyproheptadine, a first-generation antihistamine with anti-serotonergic properties. This dual mechanism of action allows it to effectively treat allergic symptoms and to stimulate appetite.
searchable.description: Cyprodine works by blocking the effects of two key natural chemical messengers in the body: histamine (released during allergic reactions) and serotonin (a chemical messenger that regulates appetite in the brain). Its antihistaminic properties provide relief from allergic symptoms, while its antiserotonergic activity in the hypothalamus (the appetite control center) makes it an effective appetite stimulant and promoter of weight gain in underweight individuals.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Cyprodine

This is often a Prescription-Only Medicine (POM). Dosage must be individualized according to the needs and response of the patient.

  • Adults (Allergy Relief): Typical initial dose is 4 mg (one tablet or 10 ml syrup) taken three times a day.
  • Adults (Appetite Stimulation): Dosages vary, typically starting at 4 mg once daily and potentially increasing under medical supervision.
  • Children (2–14 years): Dosage is usually calculated by body weight and indication. Do NOT give to children under 2 years of age.
  • Maximum Daily Dose: Do not exceed 0.5 mg/kg body weight daily for children, or 32 mg daily for adults (for allergy).

Proper Administration

  • Administration: Take orally. Tablets should be swallowed whole with water. Syrup should be measured using a dosing syringe or medicine spoon (not a household spoon).
  • Timing: Can be taken with or without food. Taking it with food may minimize gastrointestinal side effects.
  • Drowsiness: Because of the strong sedative effect, initial doses are often taken at bedtime.

Duration: Use the lowest effective dose for the shortest period necessary.
searchable.specification:
• Form: Oral Tablet (e.g., 4 mg) or Oral Syrup (e.g., 2 mg/5 ml)
  • Active Ingredient: Cyproheptadine Hydrochloride
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Cyprodine

  • Age Restriction: Do NOT give to children younger than 2 years of age.
  • Known Allergies: Do NOT use if you have a known allergy to Cyproheptadine or any other first-generation antihistamine.
  • Specific Health Issues: Do NOT use if you have narrow-angle glaucoma, a stomach ulcer, pyloroduodenal obstruction, an enlarged prostate (or other urination problems), or are experiencing an asthma attack.
  • Drug Interactions: Do NOT use if you have taken a Monoamine Oxidase Inhibitor (MAOI) in the last 14 days (e.g., Phenelzine, Selegiline).

Side Effects of Cyprodine

  • Common Side Effects (Expected): Drowsiness, sedation, dizziness, dry mouth, blurred vision, and weight gain (due to increased appetite).
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (Medical Attention Needed): Signs of severe anticholinergic effects (severe confusion, hallucinations, inability to urinate), severe allergic reaction, or paradoxical excitement (especially in young children).

Precautions and Warnings of Cyprodine

  • CNS Depression: Use caution with all other Central Nervous System (CNS) depressants, including sedatives, hypnotics, and antianxiety agents, as effects are additive.
  • Elderly Patients: Use with caution, as older adults are more susceptible to the anticholinergic side effects (dizziness, confusion, dry mouth).
  • Driving Risk: The effects on mental alertness can be significant and dangerous.
  • Breastfeeding: Generally advised not to use while breastfeeding, as antihistamines may pass into breast milk and can cause sedation in the infant or reduce milk supply.

searchable.name: D-Koff Syrup
searchable.brief_info: D-Koff Syrup is a combination medication specifically formulated to provide rapid and effective temporary relief from the symptoms of the common cold, allergies, flu, and specifically, dry, hacking coughs
searchable.description: D-Koff Syrup is a triple-action formula combines a cough suppressant, an antihistamine, and a decongestant to address multiple symptoms simultaneously. D-Koff Syrup helps clear stuffy nose and sinus discomfort while calming the irritated cough center in the brain, allowing for easier breathing and more restful comfort.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of D-Koff Syrup
Take in the dose and duration as advised by the pharmacist or physician. The dose varies based on age and body weight.

  • Children 2-6 years: Half teaspoonful (2.5 mL)
  • Children 6-12 years: One teaspoonful (5 mL)
  • Adults: Two teaspoonfuls (10 mL)

Note: These doses are typically taken 3 times a day or every 4–6 hours, not exceeding 4 doses in 24 hours. Always use the provided measuring cup/spoon.

Administration

  • Proper Administration: Shake the bottle well before use. Measure the prescribed dose accurately using a standardized measuring cup or spoon provided with the package. Do not use regular household spoons.

Best Time to Take It: D-Koff Syrup may be taken with or without food. Take with food if stomach upset occurs.
searchable.specification: Pack Size: D-Koff Syrup is commonly supplied in a 100 mL bottle.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of D-Koff Syrup
Do NOT take D-Koff Syrup if you:

  • Are allergic to Dextromethorphan, Chlorpheniramine, Phenylephrine, or any components.
  • Are currently taking or have taken a Monoamine Oxidase Inhibitor (MAOI) (certain antidepressants) within the last 14 days. This combination can cause dangerous increases in blood pressure.
  • Have severe high blood pressure (Hypertension), severe heart disease, or certain types of glaucoma (narrow-angle glaucoma).
  • Have severe liver impairment (use is generally discouraged).

Side Effects of D-Koff Syrup

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Drowsiness/Sedation, dizziness, dry mouth, nausea, headache, and nervousness.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects: Fast or irregular heartbeat (palpitations), blurred vision, mood changes (hallucinations, excitement in children), or signs of a serious allergic reaction (hives, swelling, difficulty breathing). If these occur, seek emergency medical attention immediately.

Precautions and Warnings of D-Koff Syrup

  • Before Starting: Inform your doctor if you have diabetes, high blood pressure, asthma, chronic cough (from smoking or long-term lung disease), thyroid problems (overactive thyroid), or kidney disease.
  • Pregnancy & Breastfeeding: Consult your doctor. The syrup may be unsafe to use during pregnancy (Category C) and is generally not recommended during breastfeeding as the drug may pass into breastmilk.
  • Other Drug Interactions: Use caution with other CNS depressants (like sleeping pills, anti-anxiety medications) as they can enhance drowsiness.

searchable.name: BENYLIN INFANTS GLYCEROL DAY/NIGHT COUGH
searchable.brief_info: BENYLIN is the Cough expert in speed & efficacy. It has a unique range specially formulated to treat all types of Coughs and Catarrah for the whole family.
searchable.description:
Coughing starts when parts of your child’s breathing passages become inflamed or irritated.

BENYLIN Infant’s Cough Syrup contains glycerol and is sugar free.

How it works?

The apple flavoured syrup soothes your little one’s throat and helps relieve discomfort. Soothes dry and tickly coughs for children aged 3 months and over.

What is a cough?

Coughing starts when part of your breathing passage becomes inflamed or irritated.

The cough is simply a reflex to try and remove the irritant and clear the airway. Coughs can either be productive (producing excess mucus or phlegm) or unproductive (producing no mucus or phlegm).

Chesty and mucus coughs are productive, whereas dry and tickly coughs are unproductive.

Active ingredient :Glycerol
searchable.how_to_use:
Children 3 months - 1 year: one 5ml spoonful three to four times a day.

Children 1 to 5 years: two 5ml spoonfuls three to four times a day.

Children under 3 months: not recommended.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Diabetics should take note that glycerol may affect blood sugar levels.

If symptoms persist for more than 3 days or get worse, patients should stop use and consult a doctor.

searchable.description:
About this item

  • Stay heartburn-free all day and all night
  • Blocks sources of stomach acid that cause frequent heartburn
  • Stop acid before it starts; Proton Pump Inhibitors (PPIs) like Nexium 24HR blocks acid at the source, turning your body’s acid pumps from on to off and giving you complete protection (3) from frequent heartburn
  • Nighttime Tips: Did you know that upto 75 Percent of people who suffer from frequent heartburn experience it regularly at night? (5) Nexium 24HR helps you sleep comfortably through the night by reducing interruptions due to frequent heartburn (4)
  • Daytime Tips: Help prevent heartburn during the day by avoiding smoking and overeating, sustained life stresses, and trigger foods such as spicy foods, coffee, and acidic fruits.
  • If you suffer from frequent heartburn, occurring 2 or more days a week, your daily life can be significantly impacted; Nexium 24HR all day, all night protection from frequent heartburn helps you get back to all the activities and food you love with just 1 pill a day (4)
  • Ingredients
    Active Ingredients (in each capsule)...Purpose Esomeprazole 20mg... Acid Reducer (Each delayed-released capsule corresponds to 22.3mg esomeprazole magnesium trihydrate) Inactive Ingredients: Corn Starch, D&C Red No. 28, FD&C Blue No. 1, FD&C Red No. 40, Ferric Oxide, Gelatin, Glyceryl Monostearate, Hydroxypropyl Cellulose, Hypromellose, Magnesium Stearate, Methacrylic Acid Copolymer, Pharmaceutical Ink, Polysorbate 80, Sucrose, Talc, Titanium Dioxide, Triethyl Citrate.
searchable.how_to_use: Adults 18 years of age and older. This product is to be used once a day (every 24 hours), every day for 14 days May take 1 to 4 days for full effect 14-Day Course of Treatment. Swallow 1 capsule with a glass of water before eating in the morning.
searchable.famasi_advice: Allergy Alert: Do not use if you are allergic to esomeprazole. Do not use if you have: trouble or pain swallowing food, vomiting with blood, or bloody or black stools, heartburn with lightheadedness, sweating or dizziness, chest pain or shoulder pain with shortness of breath, sweating, pain spreading to arms, neck or shoulders, or lightheadedness, frequent chest pain. These may be signs of a serious condition. See your doctor. Ask a doctor before use if you have had heartburn over 3 months, frequent wheezing, particularly with heartburn, unexplained weight loss, nausea or vomiting, stomach pain. This may be a sign of a more serious condition. Ask a doctor or pharmacist before use if you are taking warfarin, clopidogrel or cilostazol (blood-thinning medicines), prescription antifungal or anti-yeast medicines, digoxin (heart medicine), diazepam (anxiety medicine), tacrolimus or mycophenolate mofetil (immune system medicines), prescriptions antiretrovirals (medicines for HIV infection), methotrexate (arthritis medicine). Stop use and ask a doctor if, your heartburn continues or worsens, you need to take this product for more than 14 days, you need to take more than 1 course of treatment every 4 months, you get diarrhea. If pregnant or breast-feeding, ask a health professional before use. Keep out of reach of children, in case of overdose, get medical help or contact a Poison Control Center right away. Do not use if seal under bottle cap imprinted with "sealed for your protection" or yellow band around the center of each capsule is broken or missing.

searchable.name: Bevel Body Aluminum-Free Deodorant
searchable.brief_info: Bevel Aluminum-Free Deodorant is a premium grooming solution designed for those seeking effective odor control without the use of harsh chemicals. Formulated specifically to penetrate through coarse or curly hair to reach the skin, it provides a clean, streak-free application while nourishing the underarms.
searchable.description: Bevel Aluminum-Free Deodorant is a solid stick formulated to "fight the funk" using natural, skin-loving ingredients. Unlike traditional antiperspirants that block sweat glands with aluminum salts, this deodorant neutralizes odor-causing bacteria while allowing your body’s natural cooling process to function. It goes on clear, leaving no white residue or clumps on skin or clothing.
searchable.how_to_use:
Proper Administration

  • Step 1: Prep: For maximum efficacy, apply to clean, dry skin. Using a mild exfoliating body wash beforehand helps remove dead skin cells and bacteria.
  • Step 2: Application: Smooth a thin, even layer directly onto the underarms. 3–4 swipes are typically sufficient for full coverage.
  • Step 3: Dry: Allow a few seconds for the product to set before dressing to ensure maximum absorption.

The "Transition" Period If you are switching from an aluminum-based antiperspirant, your body may undergo a "detox" phase for 1–4 weeks. During this time, you may experience temporary increased sweating or a change in odor as your pores "unclog." This is normal and will subside once your skin chemistry balances.
searchable.specification:
• Size: 2.5 oz (70g) Solid Stick
  • Form: Clear Solid
  • Free From: Aluminum, Parabens, Phthalates, Sulfates, Alcohol, and Talc.
  • Skin Type: Suitable for all skin types, including sensitive skin.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications

  • Known Sensitivities: Discontinue use if you experience a persistent rash or redness. Some individuals may be sensitive to baking soda (sodium bicarbonate) if present in the specific scent variant.

Precautions

  • Not an Antiperspirant: This product does not stop you from sweating; it stops the sweat from smelling. If you are a heavy sweater, you will still experience natural moisture.
  • Post-Shaving: Avoid applying immediately after shaving or if you have broken skin/nicks to prevent stinging.
  • Avoid Eyes: Keep away from eyes and mucous membranes.

searchable.name: Pregnacare Plus Omega-3 X 56
searchable.brief_info: Pregnacare Plus Omega-3 is a specialized dual-pack nutritional supplement designed to provide essential support for both mother and baby throughout pregnancy. It combines the trusted Pregnacare multi-nutrient tablet with a high-purity Omega-3 capsule containing DHA. This comprehensive formula is expertly balanced to ensure that you are receiving the recommended levels of Folic Acid and Vitamin D, alongside essential fatty acids that contribute to the normal development of your baby’s brain and eyes.
searchable.description: •  Pregnancy significantly increases your body's nutritional requirements. Pregnacare Plus offers an easy way to safeguard your diet. The pack contains 28 prenatal multivitamin tablets and 28 Omega-3 capsules (totaling 56 per pack). This combination replaces the need for other multivitamins and ensures that both the mother's health and the baby's structural development are supported with premium-grade ingredients.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage and Administration

  • Dosage: Take one (1) tablet (green blister) and one (1) capsule (pink blister) per day.
  • Method: Swallow each whole with water or a cold drink. Do not chew.
  • Timing: Take these during or immediately after your main meal. This is crucial for the absorption of the Omega-3 and fat-soluble vitamins, and it significantly reduces the likelihood of feeling nauseous.

Proper Usage Tips

  • Consistency: Take your supplements at the same time each day (e.g., right after breakfast) to make it a habit.
  • Full Stomach: Never take these on an empty stomach; pregnancy can make your digestion sensitive, and the vitamins are better tolerated with food.
  • No Multi-Vitamins: This formula is complete. Do not take any other multivitamin supplements while taking Pregnacare Plus unless directed by your doctor.

How to Store and Dispose of Pregnacare Plus

  • Storage: Store in a cool, dry place below 25°C. Keep the blisters in the outer carton to protect them from light.
  • Disposal: Keep out of reach of children. Dispose of empty packaging in your regular household recycling.
searchable.specification:
• Pack Size: 56 (28 Tablets + 28 Capsules).
  • Form: Oral Tablets and Soft-gel Capsules.
  • Manufacturer: Vitabiotics (UK).
searchable.famasi_advice:
Safety and Precautions

Contraindications and Restrictions

  • Allergy Information: The capsules contain Fish Oil. The tablets contain Soya. Do not use if you have these allergies.
  • Blood Thinners: If you are taking oral anticoagulants (like Warfarin), consult your doctor before taking these capsules, as Omega-3 may have a mild effect on blood thinning.

Side Effects of Pregnacare Plus

  • Common: Occasional mild nausea or a "fishy" aftertaste (taking the capsule with a cold drink and food minimizes this).
  • Note: This product contains Iron, which can cause a harmless darkening of the stool.
  • Action: If you experience any severe stomach pain or an unusual rash, stop use and contact your midwife or doctor.

Precautions and Warnings

  • Iron Content: This product contains Iron, which can be harmful to very young children if taken in excess. Always keep out of reach of children.
  • Food Supplement: These are intended to supplement a healthy diet, not replace it. Ensure you are also eating a varied diet rich in leafy greens and proteins.

searchable.name: Nurofen Express 400mg
searchable.brief_info: Nurofen Express is a high-strength, fast-acting analgesic designed to provide rapid relief from intense pain and inflammation. Unlike standard tablets that need time to dissolve in the stomach, Nurofen Express contains Ibuprofen (400mg) in a liquid gel format. This allows the medicine to be absorbed into the bloodstream up to twice as fast as standard ibuprofen tablets, reaching the site of pain quickly when you need it most.
searchable.description: Nurofen Express utilizes specialized "Liquid Capsule" technology. The active ingredient is already dissolved in a liquid center, which is released immediately once the capsule reaches the stomach. This makes it an ideal choice for acute, sharp pains that require urgent attention, such as migraines or dental emergencies.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage and Administration

  • Adults and Children over 12 years: Take one (1) capsule with water.
  • Frequency: If needed, take one capsule every 4 to 6 hours.
  • Maximum Dose: Do not exceed 3 capsules (1200mg) in any 24-hour period.
  • Note: This 400mg strength is high-potency; usually, one capsule is sufficient for relief.

Proper Usage Tips

  • Take with Food: IMPORTANT: To protect your stomach lining, always take Nurofen Express with food or a glass of milk.
  • Swallow Whole: Do not chew or break the liquid capsules; they are designed to be swallowed whole for optimal absorption.
  • Short-Term Use: Use the lowest effective dose for the shortest time necessary. If symptoms persist for more than 3 days (fever) or 10 days (pain), consult your doctor.

How to Store and Dispose of Nurofen Express

  • Storage: Store in a cool, dry place below 25°C. Keep the capsules in their original blister pack to protect them from moisture.
  • Disposal: Keep out of reach of children. Return unused capsules to your local pharmacy for safe disposal.
searchable.specification:
• Pack Size: Often available in packs of 10 or 20 liquid capsules.
  • Strength: 400mg per capsule.
  • Manufacturer: Reckitt Benckiser.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Safety and Precautions

Contraindications and Restrictions

  • Stomach Issues: Do not use if you have an active stomach ulcer, a history of stomach bleeding, or perforation.
  • Allergy: Do not use if you are allergic to Ibuprofen, Aspirin, or other NSAIDs.
  • Heart/Kidney Health: Consult a doctor if you have severe heart failure, high blood pressure, or kidney disease.

Side Effects of Nurofen Express

  • Common: Indigestion, heartburn, or nausea (less likely if taken with food).
  • Serious (Rare): Signs of stomach bleeding (black stools), skin rash, or sudden wheezing.
  • Action: If you experience any allergic reactions or severe abdominal pain, stop taking the medication and seek medical help immediately.

Precautions and Warnings

  • Asthma: Some asthma sufferers may find that NSAIDs trigger a "flare-up." Use with caution.
  • Pregnancy: Do not take Nurofen during the last 3 months of pregnancy. Consult your doctor if you are in the first 6 months or breastfeeding.
  • Avoid Duplication: Do not take this with other NSAIDs (like Naproxen or Diclofenac) or Aspirin, as this significantly increases the risk of side effects.

searchable.name: Lactulose solution
searchable.brief_info: EXPIRY DATE: MAY 2023. Lactulose oral solution is used to treat constipation. It’s also used to treat a brain problem called portal-systemic encephalopathy.
searchable.description:
Lactulose oral solution is used to treat constipation. It’s also used to treat a brain problem called portal-systemic encephalopathy. This problem is a complication of severe liver disease.

This drug may be used as part of a combination therapy.

Lactulose belongs to a class of drugs called laxatives. A class of drugs is a group of medications that work in a similar way. These drugs are often used to treat similar conditions.

Lactulose is a synthetic (man-made) sugar. It breaks down in your large intestine and then draws water into the intestine. This softens your stool, which helps ease constipation.

Active ingredients: Lactulose

 Side effects of lactulose

burping
gas
nausea
cramps anywhere in the body
searchable.how_to_use:
Adult dosage (ages 18 years and older)

Typical dosage: 1–2 tablespoons (or 15–30 mL) once per day.
Maximum dosage: 4 tablespoons (60 mL) per day.

Child dosage (ages 0–17 years)

Starting dosage: 2.5–10 mL taken by mouth each day in three or four divided doses.
Dosage increases for older children and adolescents: Your child’s doctor may increase your child’s dosage to 40–90 mL per day taken in three or four divided doses.
searchable.famasi_advice: Lactulose is used for short-term treatment of constipation. It’s used for short-term or long-term treatment of portal-systemic encephalopathy. It comes with serious risks if you don’t take it as prescribed.

searchable.name: Odomos Mosquito Repellent Cream 50g
searchable.brief_info: Odomos Mosquito Repellent Cream 50g is a popular and trusted topical repellent is formulated to offer up to 8 hours of effective protection against mosquitoes, including those that transmit Dengue, Malaria, and Chikungunya.
searchable.description: Odomos Mosquito Repellent Cream 50g unlike many insect repellents, Odomos is made with a non-sticky, pleasant-smelling cream base and uses an active ingredient that deters mosquitoes without resorting to harsh, potentially irritating chemicals like DEET. Odomos Mosquito Repellent Cream 50g is safe for use on the entire family, including children, and is an essential item for both indoor and outdoor protection, allowing you to enjoy your day worry-free.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Odomos Mosquito Repellent Cream 50g

This is a topical repellent.

  • Dosage: Apply a small, pea-sized amount sufficient to cover all exposed skin. Do not apply under clothing.

Proper Administration

  • Application: Squeeze a small amount of cream onto the palm. Rub your hands together and apply the cream evenly and sparingly to all exposed areas of the skin.
  • Best Time to Use: Apply before going outdoors or at the beginning of the evening, or whenever mosquito activity is expected.
  • Reapplication: Reapply after approximately 6 to 8 hours, or immediately after excessive sweating or washing the treated area.

Face Application: Apply to the face sparingly, avoiding the immediate eye and mouth areas.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Odomos Mosquito Repellent Cream 50g

  • Known Allergies: Do NOT use if you have a known allergy or hypersensitivity to any of the ingredients, including the active repellent compound or perfume.
  • Damaged Skin: Avoid applying to cuts, wounds, burns, irritated skin, or rashes.

Side Effects of Odomos Mosquito Repellent Cream 50g

Side effects are rare when used externally as directed.

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Temporary, mild skin irritation or localized redness, especially if applied excessively or to sensitive areas.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (That May Require Medical Attention): Signs of severe allergic reaction (rash, hives, swelling, difficulty breathing). If severe skin irritation or blistering occurs, discontinue use immediately and seek medical advice.

Precautions and Warnings of Odomos Mosquito Repellent Cream 50g

  • External Use Only: This product is for external application to the skin only. Do not ingest.
  • Avoid Sensitive Areas: Do not apply to the eyes, lips, nose, or mouth. If contact occurs, rinse thoroughly with water.
  • Application to Children: When applying to children, apply the cream to your own hands first and then apply to the child's exposed skin. Do not apply directly to a child's hands or fingers, as they may put their hands in their mouths or eyes.
  • Storage and Disposal: Close the tube tightly after use. Wash treated skin with soap and water once you are back indoors and no longer need protection.
  • Sunscreen: If using sunscreen, apply the sunscreen first, allow it to absorb fully for 20 minutes, and then apply the Odomos repellent.

Over The Counter (OTC): Adult women:

  • Take both components daily:

    • 1 Omega-3 Capsule

searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Wellwoman Plus

Do NOT take Wellwoman Plus if you have:

  • Drug Allergies: Known hypersensitivity or allergy to fish, soya, or any other ingredients.

Side Effects of Wellwoman Plus

Precautions and Warnings of Wellwoman Plus

Iron Intake: The product contains iron. Keep safely out of reach of children.

searchable.name: CYPROHEPTADINE (CYPRIVIT) SYRUP 200MLS
searchable.brief_info: Cyprivite Syrup is a combination medicine used to treat loss of appetite. It is an effective appetite stimulant. It works by reducing the effect of a chemical messenger which regulates appetite.
searchable.description: CYPROHEPTADINE (CYPRIVIT) SYRUP 200MLS is a trusted therapeutic formulation used primarily to stimulate appetite and treat various allergic conditions.   • Adults: Usually 4mg (10ml) three times daily.
  • Children: Dosage is based on age and weight.14 Take as directed by the pharmacist or physician.

How to Store and Dispose

Store in a cool, dry place below 30°C, away from direct sunlight. Keep the bottle tightly closed. Dispose of unused syrup via a pharmacy take-back program; do not pour it down the drain.

Quick Tips

  • Take the syrup before meals to maximize appetite stimulation.
  • Use a calibrated measuring cup for accurate dosing.
  • Increase water intake to combat "dry mouth" side effects.15
  • Pair with a calorie-dense diet for the best weight-gain results.
searchable.specification:
• Volume: 200ml.
  • Form: Liquid Syrup.
  • Packaging: Amber glass/plastic bottle in a carton.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of CYPROHEPTADINE (CYPRIVIT) SYRUP 200MLS

  • Age: Do not use in newborn or premature infants.8
  • Health Conditions: Contraindicated in patients with narrow-angle glaucoma, stenosing peptic ulcer, or prostatic hypertrophy.9
  • Lactation: Not recommended for breastfeeding mothers as it may inhibit lactation or pass into breast milk.10

Side Effects of CYPROHEPTADINE (CYPRIVIT) SYRUP 200MLS

  • Common: Drowsiness, sedation, dry mouth, and blurred vision.11
  • Rare: Confusion, palpitations, or excitation (especially in children).
  • Action: If severe dizziness or difficulty urinating occurs, stop use and consult a healthcare provider.

Precautions and Warnings

  • Drowsiness: Avoid driving or operating machinery while using this syrup.12
  • Alcohol: Do not consume alcohol as it increases the sedative effect.13

The 10-count pack is perfect for those who prioritize maximum sensation and comfort during intimate moments. Because they contain no natural rubber latex, they offer a comfortable fit that transmits body heat more effectively than standard latex, providing a more intimate and realistic experience.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage and Administration

This is a single-use device. Use a fresh condom for every act of intercourse.

  • Administration: Carefully open the foil wrapper. Check that the condom is right-side out. Before contact, place it on the head of the erect penis. Pinch the tip to expel air and roll it down to the base.
  • After Use: Hold the condom at the base during withdrawal to prevent slipping. Wrap and dispose of in the bin.

Compatibility: Compatible with water-based and silicone-based lubricants. Never use oil-based products (like petroleum jelly or lotions) with non-latex polyisoprene, as they can cause the material to fail.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications

  • Oil-Based Lubes: Do not use in conjunction with oil-based lubricants or topical vaginal medications that contain oil.

Side Effects

  • Common side effects: Possible localized irritation if sensitive to silicone lubricant.
  • Serious side effects: Rare allergic reactions to polyisoprene. Discontinue use if swelling or a rash occurs.

Precautions

  • Electronic Testing: Each condom is 100% electronically tested for quality assurance.
  • Emergency: If the condom slips or breaks, consult a pharmacist or doctor within 72 hours for advice on emergency contraception.

searchable.name: SKYN ORIGINAL Non-Latex Condoms, 10 Count
searchable.brief_info: SKYN Original Non-Latex Condoms are the world’s first premium condoms made from polyisoprene. Designed for people who want to protect their health without the rubbery smell or texture of latex, these condoms offer a natural, skin-to-skin feel that is both incredibly soft and strong.
searchable.description: SKYN Original Non-Latex Condoms are the founding product of the SKYN revolution. Made from SKYNFEEL™, a high-tech non-latex material, they provide a much softer and more natural sensation than traditional latex condoms. This 10-count pack is perfect for those who require reliable protection but have a latex allergy or skin sensitivity. The material is strong enough to offer peace of mind while being flexible enough to transmit body heat and motion, creating a more intimate experience for both partners.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage and Administration

This is a single-use device. A new condom must be used for every act of intercourse.

  • Administration: Open the wrapper with care—do not use teeth or scissors. Verify that the condom is right-side out. Place on the head of the erect penis before any intimate contact occurs. Pinch the reservoir tip to remove air and roll the condom down to the base.
  • After Intimacy: Hold the base of the condom during withdrawal while the penis is still erect to avoid spilling.

Compatibility: SKYN condoms are compatible ONLY with water-based and silicone-based lubricants. NEVER use oil-based products (like baby oil, body lotion, or massage oils), as they weaken polyisoprene and will cause the condom to burst.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications

  • Oil Sensitivity: Do not use if either partner is using oil-based vaginal or rectal medications, as these will degrade the condom material.

Side Effects

  • Common Side Effects: Localized irritation if one partner is sensitive to the silicone lubricant.
  • Serious Side Effects: Rare allergic reactions to polyisoprene. Discontinue use if significant swelling, rash, or itching occurs.

Precautions

  • Electronic Testing: Every single condom is electronically tested to ensure it meets global safety and durability standards.
  • Emergency: If the condom breaks or slips, consult a doctor or pharmacist within 72 hours to discuss emergency contraception options.

searchable.name: Enhanz Capsules
searchable.brief_info: Enhanz Capsules are a powerful non-steroidal vitamin and mineral formula designed to help adult men improve natural testosterone levels, enhance muscle strength, boost vitality, and support reproductive health.
searchable.description: Enhanz Capsules are a unique softgel supplement featuring a synergistic blend of minerals, B vitamins, and L-Carnitine, often referred to as a non-steroidal formula for vitality and endurance. The core of its efficacy lies in the patented ZMA powder (Zinc, Magnesium, and Vitamin B6), which is vital for hormone regulation and muscle function. Unlike simple multivitamins, Enhanz Capsules are specifically engineered to optimize metabolic processes and increase the levels of sexual hormones, which in turn aids in building lean muscle mass when combined with exercise, and significantly improves stamina and physical performance. The formula often includes L-OptiZinc, a patented form of Zinc that is absorbed better and retained longer than standard zinc supplements.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Enhanz Capsules

Enhanz Capsules is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) Dietary Supplement.

Take 2 to 3 capsules once daily, or as directed by a physician.

Proper Administration

  • Route: Oral capsule.
  • How to Take: Swallow the softgel capsule whole with a glass of water.
  • Timing: Typically recommended to be taken 15 to 20 minutes before bedtime to align with the body's natural cycle of hormone release and muscle recovery.

Missed Dose Guidance

  • If you miss a dose, simply resume your regular dosing schedule the following night. Do not double the dose.
searchable.specification: The active ingredients include Zinc (5.58 mg), Magnesium (83.52 mg), Vitamin B6 (1.93 mg), L-Carnitine (100 mg), Nicotinamide (20 mg), Selenium (70 mcg), and other B vitamins (B1, B2, Biotin). It is supplied as a softgel capsule. It is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) Dietary Supplement for men's health.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Enhanz Capsules

Do NOT use Enhanz Capsules if you:

  • Have a known allergy or hypersensitivity to Zinc, Magnesium, Selenium, L-Carnitine, B Vitamins, or any other component of the softgel capsule.
  • Are female. (The supplement is primarily designed for adult males due to its testosterone-boosting claims).

Pregnancy & Breastfeeding Warnings

  • General: This product is not intended for use by pregnant or breastfeeding women.

Side Effects of Enhanz Capsules

Side effects are generally rare when the product is taken at the recommended dose.

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Mild digestive upset, nausea, or cramping may occasionally occur.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (that may require medical attention):
    • Overdose Symptoms: Taking significantly more than the recommended dosage (especially high selenium levels) may cause symptoms like garlic breath, hair loss, brittle fingernails, skin inflammation, or gastric problems.
    • Allergic Reactions: Severe rash or swelling.

What to do if side effects occur:

  • For mild nausea or cramping, ensure you are taking the capsule with enough water.
  • If you suspect an allergic reaction or signs of overdose, discontinue use immediately and consult a physician.

Precautions and Warnings of Enhanz Capsules

  • Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD): Consult your doctor. The components may increase the metabolic rate, which could potentially place a burden on the kidneys. Use only after consulting a doctor.
  • Milk/Calcium Interaction: Enhanz Capsules should NOT be taken with dairy products (milk) or any other calcium-containing foods or supplements (like antacids), as calcium may significantly interfere with the absorption of the ZMA complex (Zinc and Magnesium).
  • Dosage Limit: Do not exceed the recommended daily dose of 2-3 capsules unless specifically advised by a physician.
  • Not a Meal Replacement: This product is a supplement and does not replace the need for a balanced, protein-rich diet and regular exercise.

searchable.name: Accu-check Active Machine
searchable.brief_info: The Accu-Chek Active blood glucose meter can help to make it convenient by giving you fast, hassle-free, and accurate results.
searchable.description:
The Accu-Chek Active blood glucose meter can help to make it convenient by giving you fast, hassle-free, and accurate results

Glucose meter The best glucose meters, also known as glucometers, are small handheld devices used for blood sugar testing, and are an important tool for managing diabetes. When paired with a testing strip, glucose monitors display your blood sugar level in that moment.
searchable.how_to_use:
• Wash and dry your hands—using warm water may help the blood flow
  • Turn on the meter and prepare a test strip as outlined in your owner's booklet. Many Accu-Chek meters turn on automatically when a strip is inserted.
  • Choose your spot—don't check from the same finger all the time. Using the side of the fingertip may be less painful than the pads.
  • Prepare the lancing device according to the user guide provided, then lance your fingertip or other approved site to get a drop of blood.4
  • Touch and hold the test strip opening to the drop until it has absorbed enough blood to begin the test.
  • View your test result and take the proper steps if your blood sugar is high or low, based on your healthcare professionals' recommendations.
  • Discard the used lancet properly.
  • Record the results in a logbook, hold them in the meter's memory or download to an app or computer so you can review and analyze them later.
searchable.famasi_advice: Calibrate the device regularly. Dispose of used strips and lancets safely. Consult your doctor for abnormal readings.

Denture Sores: Massage approximately one centimetre of Bonjela onto the sore area and leave at least 30 minutes before reinserting the dentures.
searchable.famasi_advice:
DO NOT GIVE TO CHILDREN OR ADOLESCENTS UNDER 16 YEARS OF AGE. This is because there is a possible link between Salicylates and Reye's syndrome when given to children. Reye's syndrome is a very rare disease which affects the brain and liver and can be fatal.

 DO NOT USE if you are suffering from a stomach ulcer .

DO NOT USE if you are allergic to salicylates or to any of the other ingredients in this product. .

 Do not apply Bonjela directly to the dentures. Do not exceed the stated dose as Salicylate toxicity may occur as a result. If your symptoms do not go away after 7 days of treatment, you should see your Doctor or Dentist.

searchable.description:
Buscopan 10mg Tablet is used in the treatment of pain due to smooth muscle spasm. It relieves cramps in the stomach, intestine, bladder and ureter. It is also used to relieve the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (symptoms include abdominal pain, cramping, bloating, and diarrhea or constipation).

Buscopan 10mg Tablet effectively relieves smooth muscle pain, stiffness or spasm, thereby improving muscle movement. It relaxes the muscles and improves the movement of food. This helps to treat cramps, pain, bloating and discomfort. 

Ingredient: Hyoscine butylbromide.

Side Effects: Most side effects do not require any medical attention and disappear as your body adjusts to the medicine. Consult your doctor if they persist or if you’re worried about them.

Common side effects includes;

  • Blurred vision
  • Increased heart rate
  • Dryness in mouth
  • Diarrhea
  • Dilatation of pupil
searchable.how_to_use:

searchable.famasi_advice: Take a fiber-rich diet, avoid oily or spicy food and drink plenty of fluids while taking it to make sure you stay hydrated. Eventually, it will help you to go about your daily activities more easily and have a better, more active, quality of life.

searchable.name: Clearblue Digital Ovulation test kit
searchable.brief_info: Clearblue Connected Ovulation Test System is clinically proven to double your chances of getting pregnant in the first cycle of use.
searchable.description:
About this item

  • Clearblue Connected Ovulation Test System is clinically proven to double your chances of getting pregnant in the first cycle of use
  • Clearblue Connected Ovulation Test System is the first and only test system to track two unique hormones and connect to your phone via Bluetooth technology with smart reminders based on your personal cycles. Typically identifies four or more fertile days.
  • Over 99% accurate in detecting the LH surge
  • Clearblue Connected app works with most iPhone or Android phones with Bluetooth 4 0,BLE
  • Smart reminders based on your personal cycles
  • 40 Clearblue Connected Ovulation; Test Sticks included. Easy to read digital results

Ingredient:1 test holder, 40 test sticks, 1 wake-up stick, and 1 English insert
searchable.how_to_use:
• Take out and unwrap the test stick. The test stick will click into place and you’ll see an indicator that says “Test Ready” when it is time to perform the test.  You’ll need to take the test stick and hold it in your urinary stream for about 5 to 7 seconds.
  • If you feel uncomfortable peeing directly on the stick, you can collect the urine in a clean container and place the absorbent part of the test stick into the collected urine for 15 seconds before taking it out. Discard the urine after you have immersed the stick into the urine sample.
  • You need to keep the absorbent part of the test stick facing downward or lay the test stick on a flat surface. After 20-40 seconds have passed, you’ll see a flashing of the “Test Ready” symbol that indicates the test is working. Don’t eject the test stick and instead, you need to put the cap back on the absorbent part of the stick and wipe off any urine that is remaining on the stick.
  • Wait for about three minutes, during which time the urine will pass into the testing chamber and the level of LH will be determined. If, after three minutes, you see nothing in the indicator window, it means that you aren’t having an LH surge.  You need to use a new test stick the next day at the same time of the day you took the test today.
  • If instead you see a smiley face in the test window, it means the test kit has determined your LH levels are high enough to be detected and you are having your LH surge.  As mentioned, once you see the smiley face, you can stop testing and plan on having sex at least once after the test becomes positive.
searchable.famasi_advice:
The test kit consists of several test sticks to be used once a day until you detect your LH surge.  

Don’t try to use more than one test strip per day as this can be confusing and won’t help you determine your LH surge any better than just testing once per day at the same time every day.

searchable.name: Exiplon Adult Cough Syrup
searchable.brief_info: Exiplon Expectorant is a comprehensive oral treatment formulated to relieve productive (chesty) coughs and respiratory congestion. By combining an expectorant, a decongestant, and an antihistamine, it targets the three main symptoms of a chest cold: thick mucus, blocked nasal passages, and allergic-type irritation. It is designed to help clear the airways, making breathing easier and coughing more productive.
searchable.description: Exiplon Expectorant is a multi-symptom linctus that addresses the "heavy" feeling in the chest associated with the common cold, bronchitis, and flu. It works by thinning out the sticky mucus (phlegm) trapped in the bronchial tubes while simultaneously shrinking swollen tissues in the nose. The addition of an antihistamine helps reduce catarrh (excess mucus production) and calms the sneezing and itching often triggered by respiratory irritants.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Exiplon Expectorant Take as directed by the pharmacist or physician.

  • Adults and Children over 12 years: 10ml to 15ml, three times daily.
  • Children (6–12 years): 5ml to 10ml, three times daily.
  • Children (2–6 years): 2.5ml to 5ml, as prescribed by a medical professional.

Proper Administration

  • Administration: Use the provided measuring cup or a medicine spoon for accuracy.
  • Hydration: Drink a full glass of water with each dose. Increased fluid intake is essential to help the Guaifenesin thin the mucus effectively.
  • Food Interaction: Can be taken with or without food.
  • Missed Dose: Take as soon as you remember, unless it is nearly time for your next dose. Do not double the dose.

How to Store and Dispose of Exiplon Expectorant

  • Storage: Store in a cool, dry place below 30°C. Protect from light. Keep the bottle tightly closed.
  • Disposal: Keep out of reach of children. Return unused or expired syrup to your local pharmacy for safe disposal.

Quick Tips for Exiplon Expectorant

  • Water is Your Friend: The expectorant works much better when you are well-hydrated.
  • Avoid Drowsiness Risks: The antihistamine in Exiplon can cause sleepiness. Avoid driving or operating machinery until you know how it affects you.
  • Short-Term Use: If symptoms persist for more than 7 days, or if the cough returns or is accompanied by a high fever, consult a doctor.
  • Avoid Alcohol: Alcohol can significantly increase the sedative effects of this medication.
searchable.specification:
• Pack Size: 100ml Bottle.
  • Form: Oral Liquid (Syrup).
  • Key Ingredients: Guaifenesin, Phenylephrine HCl, Chlorpheniramine Maleate.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Safety and Precautions

Contraindications of Exiplon Expectorant

  • MAOIs: Do NOT use if you are taking or have taken a Monoamine Oxidase Inhibitor (antidepressant) in the last 14 days.
  • Severe Hypertension: Not recommended for individuals with severe high blood pressure or coronary artery disease.
  • Known Allergies: Do NOT use if you are allergic to Guaifenesin, Phenylephrine, or Chlorpheniramine.

Side Effects of Exiplon Expectorant

  • Common: Drowsiness, dizziness, dry mouth, or mild nausea.
  • Serious but Rare: Rapid heartbeat (palpitations), difficulty urinating (especially in men with enlarged prostates), or skin rash.
  • Action: If you experience extreme nervousness or a racing heart, stop use and see a doctor.

Precautions and Warnings

  • Underlying Conditions: Use with caution if you have diabetes, hyperthyroidism, glaucoma, or heart disease.
  • Chronic Cough: Not intended for coughs caused by asthma, smoking, or emphysema unless specifically directed.
  • Pregnancy & Breastfeeding: Consult your healthcare provider before use to assess the risk-to-benefit ratio.

searchable.name: Exiplon Children cough syrup
searchable.brief_info: Exiplon Children’s Cough Syrup is a specially formulated linctus designed to relieve productive (chesty) coughs and nasal congestion in children. By combining a gentle expectorant with an antihistamine, it helps to loosen stubborn phlegm and dry up the "runny nose" (catarrh) that often accompanies childhood respiratory infections. It is a trusted choice for helping children breathe more easily and rest comfortably during a cold.
searchable.description: Exiplon Children’s Cough Syrup targets the discomfort of a "wet" cough where mucus or phlegm is present in the chest. Unlike suppressants that simply stop the cough, this syrup helps the child's natural defense by thinning out thick secretions, making them easier to expel. Simultaneously, the antihistamine component provides relief from allergic triggers and reduces excess mucus production in the nose and throat, helping to clear the child's airways.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Exiplon Children’s Cough Syrup Take as directed by the pharmacist or physician.

  • Children 6 to 12 years: 5ml to 10ml, three to four times daily.
  • Children 2 to 6 years: 2.5ml to 5ml, as prescribed by a healthcare professional.
  • Infants under 2 years: Not recommended unless specifically instructed by a pediatrician.

Proper Administration

  • Administration: Always use the measuring cup or oral syringe provided with the pack to ensure an accurate dose.
  • Hydration: Encourage the child to drink plenty of water or clear fluids; hydration is essential for the expectorant (Guaifenesin) to work effectively.
  • Food Interaction: Can be taken with or without food. If the child has a sensitive stomach, giving the syrup with a light snack may help.
  • Missed Dose: Give the dose as soon as you remember. If it is nearly time for the next dose, skip the missed one. Never double the dose.

How to Store and Dispose of Exiplon Children’s Cough Syrup

  • Storage: Store in a cool, dry place below 30°C. Protect from direct sunlight. Ensure the cap is closed tightly.
  • Disposal: Keep out of reach of children. Return any unused or expired syrup to a pharmacy for safe disposal.

Quick Tips for Exiplon Children’s

  • Monitor Sleep: The antihistamine may cause mild drowsiness, which can be beneficial if a cough is preventing the child from sleeping at night.
  • Watch for Fever: If the cough is accompanied by a persistent high fever or a rash, consult a doctor immediately.
  • Short Duration: Use for the shortest time needed. If symptoms persist for more than 5 days, seek medical advice.
  • Check Ingredients: Ensure you are not giving other medicines containing antihistamines at the same time.
searchable.specification:
• Pack Size: 100ml Bottle.
  • Form: Oral Liquid (Syrup).
  • Active Ingredients: Guaifenesin and Chlorpheniramine Maleate.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Safety and Precautions

Contraindications of Exiplon Children’s Cough Syrup

  • Known Allergies: Do NOT use if the child is allergic to Guaifenesin, Chlorpheniramine, or any of the inactive ingredients.
  • Underlying Conditions: Not suitable for children with certain rare metabolic conditions or narrow-angle glaucoma.
  • Drug Interactions: Do not use if the child is taking other sedative medications or has taken an MAOI (antidepressant) in the last 14 days.

Side Effects of Exiplon Children’s Cough Syrup

  • Common: Mild drowsiness, dry mouth, or stomach upset.
  • Rare: Occasionally, some children may experience "paradoxical excitement" (becoming hyperactive or restless) instead of drowsiness.
  • Action: If you notice a skin rash, swelling, or if the child becomes unusually agitated, stop use and contact a doctor.

Precautions and Warnings

  • Chronic Cough: Do not use for a persistent or chronic cough such as that associated with asthma, unless directed by a doctor.
  • Diabetes: Be aware that many syrups contain sugar; check with your pharmacist if the child has specific dietary requirements.
  • Supervision: Always supervise the administration to prevent accidental overdosage.

searchable.name: Sorepills Lonzenges (Honey & Lemon Flavour) x24
searchable.brief_info: Sorepills Lozenges (Honey & Lemon Flavour) x24 offer fast, soothing, and effective relief from the discomfort of sore throats and minor mouth infections.
searchable.description: Sorepills Lozenges (Honey & Lemon Flavour) contains a powerful combination of two mild antiseptic ingredients that work locally to target the bacteria associated with throat discomfort. The action of slowly dissolving the lozenge allows the active agents to work directly at the site of the infection, providing symptomatic relief while lubricating the painful area. Enhanced with the natural soothing flavor of Honey and zesty Lemon, Sorepills Lozenges are a convenient and trusted Over-The-Counter (OTC) medicine for combating the first signs of a niggly sore throat and mouth irritation.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Sorepills Lozenges (Honey & Lemon Flavour) 

This is an Over-The-Counter (OTC) medicine.

  • Adults and Children over 6 years old: Dissolve one lozenge slowly in the mouth every 2 to 3 hours.
  • Maximum Dosage: Do not exceed a maximum of 12 lozenges in 24 hours.

Proper Administration

  • Administration: The lozenge must be dissolved slowly in the mouth to allow the active ingredients to work effectively in the throat area. Do not chew or swallow the lozenge whole.
  • Best Time to Take: Take a lozenge when the throat pain or irritation is most severe.
  • Missed Dose Guidance: Do not take a double dose to make up for a forgotten lozenge. Continue to use the medicine normally.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Sorepills Lozenges (Honey & Lemon Flavour)

  • Known Allergies: Do NOT take if you are allergic (hypersensitive) to 2,4-dichlorobenzyl alcohol, amylmetacresol, or any of the other ingredients, including honey or flavouring components.
  • Age Restriction: Not suitable for children under 6 years old. Young children can choke on lozenges.
  • Sugar Intolerance: Patients with rare hereditary problems of fructose intolerance, glucose-galactose malabsorption, or sucrose-isomaltose insufficiency should not take this medicine due to the sucrose and glucose content.

Side Effects of Sorepills Lozenges (Honey & Lemon Flavour)

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): None are typically common.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (That May Require Medical Attention): Hypersensitivity reactions (allergic reactions) such as rash, hives (urticaria), or swelling of the face, neck, tongue, or throat (angioedema), which could affect breathing. Also report persistent itching or severe oral discomfort. If any of these occur, stop use immediately and consult a doctor or pharmacist.
  • Overdosage: Overdosage should not present a problem other than gastrointestinal discomfort. Treatment should be symptomatic.

Precautions and Warnings of Sorepills Lozenges (Honey & Lemon Flavour) 

  • Duration of Use: Consult a doctor or pharmacist if symptoms persist, have not improved, or have worsened after 3 days of taking the lozenges.
  • Diabetes: This medicine contains sucrose (sugar) and glucose. Patients with diabetes mellitus must account for this carbohydrate content in their diet plan.
  • Pregnancy and Breastfeeding: There is limited data on the use of these active ingredients during pregnancy or lactation. As a precaution, consult a doctor or pharmacist before using this product if you are pregnant, planning to become pregnant, or breastfeeding.
  • Administration: Dissolve the lozenge slowly in the mouth; do not chew or swallow it whole.

searchable.name: De-deons syrup 280mL
searchable.brief_info: De-deon's Syrup is a widely recognized Over-the-Counter (OTC) hematinic (blood tonic) specifically formulated to combat anemia and chronic fatigue.
searchable.description: De-deon's Syrup is a brownish-red, homogeneous syrup with a characteristic odor and taste, often known for its formulation using organic sources of iron and vitamins, contrasting with some inorganic iron supplements. De-deon's Syrup provides key nutritional elements required for blood regeneration, aiming to restore vitality and physical endurance in states of deficiency.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of De-deon's Syrup

Take as directed by the pharmacist or physician.

  • Adults: 10 mL to 20 mL twice a day.
  • Children 6 to 12 years: 10 mL twice a day.
  • Children 2 to 5 years: 5 mL twice a day.

Proper Administration

  • Administration: Swallow the syrup orally. Use a measuring spoon or cup for accurate dosing.
  • Best Time to Take It: Preferably with a meal to enhance absorption and minimize any potential stomach upset.
searchable.specification: Pack Size: De-deon's Syrup is commonly supplied in amber glass bottles of 150 mL and 280 mL.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of De-deon's Syrup

Do NOT take De-deon's Syrup if you:

  • Drug Allergies: Are hypersensitive or allergic to any of the ingredients, including Vitamin B 12, Haemoglobin, or Liver Extract.
  • Iron Overload: Have a condition characterized by too much iron in the body, such as hemochromatosis or other iron metabolism disorders.
  • Specific Anemias: If your anemia is not due to iron or B 12 deficiency (e.g., severe non-nutritional anemias), consult a specialist.

Side Effects of De-deon's Syrup

Side effects are rare when used at the recommended dosage.

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Stomach upset or pain, mild constipation or diarrhea, nausea, or vomiting. Darkening of stools is expected.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (Requires Medical Attention): Signs of severe fluid retention or heart issues (Pulmonary edema, Congestive heart failure), itching, a feeling of swelling of the entire body, or nervousness/anxiousness.
  • What to do if side effects occur: If you experience symptoms of heart failure, severe or persistent diarrhea, or a widespread allergic reaction, discontinue use and seek professional medical assistance immediately.

Precautions and Warnings of De-deon's Syrup

  • Before Starting De-deon's Syrup: Inform your doctor if you have any pre-existing medical conditions. Since it contains a rich source of iron, its use must be monitored in patients with potential iron accumulation risks.
  • Interactions: Although specific interaction studies have not been widely performed, interactions with other supplements containing iron or B 12 are possible. Avoid concurrent use of other hematic tonics unless directed by a physician.
  • Breastfeeding: The active ingredients are excreted into breast milk. While no adverse effects have been shown in breastfed infants, use should still be under the supervision of a doctor.

searchable.name: Nature's Field Toxi-Flush Tablets
searchable.brief_info: Nature's Field Toxi-Flush is a potent, non-stimulant herbal formula trusted for promoting the natural detoxification and cleansing of the digestive tract, liver, colon, and lymph system, enhancing the elimination of accumulated metabolic wastes and environmental toxins.
searchable.description: Nature's Field Toxi-Flush Tablets are an advanced dietary supplement formulated to support the body's complex detoxification pathways. In modern life, the body is constantly exposed to toxins from food, water, processed items, and the environment. These toxins can lead to physical and mental health issues like chronic fatigue, headaches, and insomnia. This product features a synergistic blend of powerful herbal extracts designed to support the liver (the body's main filter), enhance bile production, and promote the flushing of wastes through the colon and kidneys. Crucially, Toxi-Flush does not contain harsh stimulant laxatives, relying instead on natural ingredients to aid the efficient, natural elimination of toxins and metabolic waste.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Nature's Field Toxi-Flush Tablets

Nature's Field Toxi-Flush Tablets is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) Dietary Supplement.

Take 2 tablets once daily, or as directed by a physician.

Proper Administration

  • Route: Oral tablet.
  • How to Take: Swallow the tablets whole with a full glass of water. Do not crush or chew.
  • Timing: Take once daily, preferably with a main meal (e.g., breakfast or dinner) to aid absorption of fat-soluble components (Milk Thistle/Turmeric) and maximize digestive comfort.
  • Duration: Typically taken for a cleansing cycle (e.g., 30 days) as advised by a health professional.

Missed Dose Guidance

  • If you miss a dose, simply take the next scheduled dose as usual the following day. Do not take a double dose.
searchable.specification: The active ingredients include Artichoke Leaf Extract, Milk Thistle Extract, Dandelion Root Powder, Aloe Leaf Powder, and Turmeric Root Powder. It is supplied as an oral tablet, typically in a bottle of 60 tablets. It is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) Dietary Supplement for multi-organ detoxification.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Nature's Field Toxi-Flush Tablets

Do NOT take Nature's Field Toxi-Flush Tablets if you:

  • Have a known allergy or hypersensitivity to Milk Thistle, Artichoke, Dandelion, Turmeric, Aloe, or any other ingredient in the tablets.
  • Have severe kidney disease or kidney stone formation (Dandelion acts as a diuretic).
  • Have a blockage of the bile duct (due to the bile-stimulating effect of Artichoke).
  • Are experiencing abdominal pain, nausea, or vomiting (consult a doctor first).

Pregnancy & Breastfeeding Warnings

  • Pregnancy: Consult your doctor. Due to the nature of detoxification and lack of clinical safety data for the high concentration of herbal extracts, use during pregnancy is advised only under the strict guidance of a healthcare professional.
  • Breastfeeding: Consult your doctor. Use only under medical supervision.

Side Effects of Nature's Field Toxi-Flush Tablets

Side effects are generally mild and related to the cleansing process.

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Increased urination (due to the diuretic effect of Dandelion), mild gastrointestinal discomfort, slight nausea, or temporary changes in stool consistency.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (that may require medical attention):
    • Severe Allergic Reactions: Rash, hives, swelling, or difficulty breathing (anaphylaxis).
    • Electrolyte Imbalance/Dehydration: Can occur with high fluid loss (diuresis/diarrhoea) if hydration is inadequate.
    • Herb-Drug Interactions: Potential interference with medications metabolized by the liver.

What to do if side effects occur:

  • For mild nausea or increased urination, ensure you are drinking sufficient water.
  • If severe diarrhoea occurs, reduce the dose and increase fluid intake.
  • For any signs of a severe allergic reaction, discontinue use immediately and seek emergency medical attention.

Precautions and Warnings of Nature's Field Toxi-Flush Tablets

  • Hydration is Crucial: Significantly increase your daily water intake while taking this supplement to support the flushing and cleansing process and prevent dehydration.
  • Drug Interactions: Consult your doctor or pharmacist if you are taking any prescription medications, especially blood thinners or diabetic medications (Milk Thistle may interfere with the metabolism of these drugs).
  • Not a Meal Replacement: This supplement is intended for cleansing and is not a substitute for a balanced diet. Do not fast or severely restrict calories while using this product.

Duration of Use: Use as directed for a dedicated cleanse period; continuous, long-term use should be discussed with a healthcare professional.

Dosage of Nature's Field Stress Defence Tablets

Nature's Field Stress Defence Tablets is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) Dietary Supplement.

Proper Administration

Missed Dose Guidance

searchable.specification: The active ingredients include B-Complex Vitamins, Magnesium, L-Theanine, Rhodiola Rosea Root Extract, Chamomile Flower Extract, Passion Flower Extract, Inositol, and Griffonia Seed Extract. It is supplied as an oral tablet in a pack of 10 strips of 10 tablets (Total 100 Tablets). It is an Over-the-Counter (OTC) Dietary Supplement for stress and mood support.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Nature's Field Stress Defence Tablets

Do NOT take Nature's Field Stress Defence Tablets if you:

  • Have a known allergy or hypersensitivity to any of the ingredients (e.g., Rhodiola, Chamomile, L-Theanine).
  • Are currently taking antidepressants (especially SSRIs or MAOIs) or anti-anxiety medication, without prior medical consultation, due to the presence of 5-HTP (Griffonia Seed Extract).
  • Are taking sedatives or other medications that cause drowsiness.

Pregnancy & Breastfeeding Warnings

  • Pregnancy: Consult your doctor. Due to the presence of several potent herbal extracts and adaptogens, use during pregnancy is advised only under the strict guidance of a healthcare professional.
  • Breastfeeding: Consult your doctor. Use only under medical supervision.

Side Effects of Nature's Field Stress Defence Tablets

Side effects are generally mild.

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Mild digestive upset, slight nausea, or mild drowsiness, especially upon initial use due to the calming herbs (Chamomile, Passion Flower) and L-Theanine.
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (that may require medical attention):
    • Serotonin Syndrome Risk: While rare, combining the 5-HTP in the tablets with certain prescription antidepressants can lead to an overdose of serotonin (symptoms include agitation, rapid heart rate, high blood pressure, and diarrhoea).
    • Allergic Reactions: Persistent rash, swelling, or difficulty breathing.

What to do if side effects occur:

  • For mild drowsiness, take the tablet later in the day.
  • If you experience any severe or persistent side effects, or any signs of a severe allergic reaction or Serotonin Syndrome symptoms, discontinue use immediately and seek medical attention.

Precautions and Warnings of Nature's Field Stress Defence Tablets

  • Medication Interactions: It is CRITICAL to inform your doctor or pharmacist about this supplement if you are taking any medication for anxiety, depression, insomnia, or pain management.
  • Driving and Operating Machinery: If the tablet causes drowsiness or dizziness, do not drive or operate heavy machinery.
  • Duration of Use: Use as directed for stress management; consult a physician for long-term use.

Not a Cure: This product is a supplement for managing symptoms; it is not intended to treat clinical depression or severe anxiety disorders.

searchable.name: Piccan Teething syrup
searchable.brief_info: Piccan Teething Remedy (also known as Piccan Teething Syrup) is a trusted pediatric medication designed to provide gentle and effective relief for infants and young children experiencing the discomforts of teething. It combines a pain reliever with a mild sedative to help soothe irritability and promote rest.
searchable.description: Piccan Teething Remedy is a dual-action oral solution specifically formulated for the teething stage. Teething often causes sore gums, restlessness, and mild fever, which can disrupt a baby's sleep and feeding. This syrup works by targeting the source of the pain while providing a calming effect to reduce fussiness. It is a staple in many households for managing the "cranky" periods associated with a child's first teeth emerging.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Piccan Teething Remedy

Always follow the dosage instructions on the label or those provided by your pediatrician. Dosage is usually based on age:

  • Infants (3 months – 1 year): 2.5ml (half a teaspoon) 3 to 4 times daily.
  • Children (1 – 5 years): 5ml to 10ml (1 to 2 teaspoons) 3 to 4 times daily.
  • Children (6 years and above): 10ml to 20ml (2 to 4 teaspoons) 3 times daily.

Proper Administration

  • Interval: Allow at least 4 to 6 hours between doses.
  • Daily Limit: Do NOT give more than 4 doses in any 24-hour period.
  • Preparation: Shake the bottle well before use and use a proper measuring spoon or oral syringe.

Duration: Do not use for more than 3 days without consulting a doctor.
searchable.specification:
• Pack Size: 100ml Bottle
  • Form: Oral Syrup / Solution
  • Active Ingredients: Paracetamol 120mg and Diphenhydramine HCl 12.5mg (per 5ml)
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications

  • Allergies: Do NOT use if the child is allergic to Paracetamol, Diphenhydramine, or any of the inactive ingredients.
  • Medical Conditions: Consult a doctor before use if the child has known liver or kidney problems.
  • MAOIs: Do not use if the child is taking or has recently taken Monoamine Oxidase Inhibitors (MAOIs).

Side Effects

  • Common Side Effects: Drowsiness, dry mouth, and occasionally dizziness or constipation.
  • Serious Side Effects: Rare allergic reactions (rash, swelling, or breathing difficulties). Seek immediate medical help if these occur. Paracetamol overdose can cause serious liver damage.

Precautions and Warnings

  • Supervision: Children receiving this medication should be kept under supervision due to its sedative effects.
  • Dehydration: Ensure the child is well-hydrated, especially if they have a fever.
  • Persistent Symptoms: If the fever does not go down or the pain worsens after 3 days, stop use and see a pediatrician.

searchable.name: Alphabetic Multivitamin
searchable.brief_info: The product Alphabetic Multivitamin is a general, broad-spectrum supplement designed to cover the daily intake of a wide range of essential vitamins and minerals. It is positioned as an all-in-one nutritional safeguard for families or individuals seeking comprehensive support.
searchable.description: Alphabetic Multivitamin is a complete and balanced daily supplement providing a foundational blend of essential vitamins and minerals, often covering the alphabet of nutrients from Vitamin A to Zinc. Designed to supplement a regular diet, this formula helps fill common nutritional gaps that may arise from varied eating habits or busy, stressful lifestyles. Alphabetic Multivitamin supports fundamental biological processes, including energy production, immune function, and cellular health, making it an excellent choice for maintaining overall vitality and preventing basic nutrient deficiencies in adults and often older children.
searchable.how_to_use:
Dosage of Alphabetic Multivitamin

This is an Over-The-Counter (OTC) nutritional supplement.

  • Adults and Children 12 years and over: Take one tablet or capsule daily, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

Proper Administration

  • Administration: Swallow the tablet/capsule whole with water.
  • Best Time to Take: Take the multivitamin with a meal (e.g., breakfast or lunch). Taking it with food enhances the absorption of the fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K) and helps to prevent minor stomach upset sometimes caused by minerals like Iron.
  • Missed Dose Guidance: If you miss a dose, take the next scheduled dose as usual with your meal. Do not take a double dose.
searchable.famasi_advice:
Contraindications of Alphabetic Multivitamin

  • Known Allergies: Do NOT take if you have a known allergy or hypersensitivity to any of the ingredients.
  • Iron Overload: Avoid use if you have conditions causing iron accumulation (e.g., Haemochromatosis).
  • Hypervitaminosis: Avoid combining with other high-dose supplements containing fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K) to prevent excessive intake.

Side Effects of Alphabetic Multivitamin

  • Common Side Effects (Mild and Expected): Mild gastrointestinal upset, nausea, or bright yellow discoloration of urine (due to Riboflavin/Vitamin B2 excretion, which is harmless).
  • Serious but Rare Side Effects (That May Require Medical Attention): Severe allergic reaction (swelling, rash). Seek medical advice immediately if serious symptoms occur or if symptoms of potential overdose (severe headache, bone pain) are noted.

Precautions and Warnings of Alphabetic Multivitamin

  • Overdose Risk: Strictly adhere to the recommended daily dosage. Excessive intake of fat-soluble vitamins can be harmful due to accumulation in the body.
  • Medication Interactions: Consult a doctor or pharmacist if you are taking blood-thinning medications (anticoagulants), thyroid medication, or certain antibiotics, as vitamins (like K) and minerals (Iron, Calcium) can interfere with their action.

Pregnancy & Breastfeeding: Consult a physician before use. While essential, pregnant or breastfeeding women may need specialized prenatal formulas with adjusted levels of Folic Acid and Iron.
